The core change replaces Phases 3-4 (code generation + integration) with Phase 3 (spec synthesis with embedded brainstorm) and Phase 4 (spec-panel review with convergence loop). Phases 0-2 remain untouched.\n    16→\n    17→**Scope**: 26 requirements (17 functional, 9 non-functional) across 5 domains, modifying ~4 files with 1 new template artifact. The work is moderate in complexity but carries meaningful behavioral and contract risk because it changes orchestration semantics, downstream artifacts, and validation expectations.\n    18→\n    19→**Key architectural decisions**:\n    20→- Behavioral patterns from `sc:brainstorm` and `sc:spec-panel` are embedded inline rather than invoked as commands, keeping the pipeline non-interactive and automatable.\n    21→- The convergence loop uses state machine terminology for documentation and edge-case reasoning, implemented as a bounded conditional loop in SKILL.md.\n    22→- Failure contracts are first-class artifacts: every execution path emits a complete return contract.\n    23→\n    24→**Planning estimate**: 6-8 working days. Critical path is fully sequential due to dependency chain.\n    25→\n    26→**Adversarial provenance**: This roadmap merges Variant A (Opus-Architect, score 81) as structural backbone with targeted improvements from Variant B (Haiku-Analyzer, score 76) identified through 3-round adversarial debate (convergence 0.78). 10 of 14 diff points fully resolved; 2 partially resolved; 2 resolved by compromise.\n    27→\n    28→## Phased Implementation Plan\n    29→\n    30→### Phase 1: Template Foundation & Pre-Implementation Verification\n    31→\n    32→**Duration**: 1-2 days | **Risk**: Low\n    33→\n    34→**Objective**: Verify scope boundaries, trace downstream dependencies, and create the reusable release spec template.\n    35→\n    36→#### Pre-Implementation Verification Checklist\n    37→\n    38→Before any template work begins, complete the following (incorporated from debate D-01, D-09):\n    39→\n    40→1. **Change inventory**: Confirm the 4 files to be modified and 1 file to be created\n    41→2. **Dependency trace**: Trace all downstream consumers of the return contract (specifically `sc:roadmap` and `sc:tasklist`) and the reviewed spec artifact\n    42→3. **Regression checklist**: Confirm Phases 0-2 behavior is unchanged and no phase references old Phase 3/4 outputs\n    43→4. **Sync requirement**: Confirm `src/superclaude/` changes must be followed by `make sync-dev`\n    44→\n    45→#### Template Creation\n    46→\n    47→1. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019zpWyTdWbNWtPXRyKUvRaY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→---\n     2→name: sc-cli-portify-protocol\n     3→description: \"Full behavioral protocol for sc:cli-portify — port inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution.\"\n     4→category: development\n     5→complexity: high\n     6→allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\n     7→mcp-servers: [sequential, serena, context7, auggie-mcp]\n     8→personas: [architect, analyzer, backend]\n     9→argument-hint: \"--workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>] [--dry-run]\"\n    10→---\n    11→\n    12→# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n    13→\n    14→Convert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n    15→\n    16→## Purpose\n    17→\n    18→SuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n    19→\n    20→- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n    21→- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n    22→- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n    23→- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n    24→- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n    25→- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n    26→\n    27→The existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n    28→\n    29→## What Gets Generated\n    30→\n    31→A CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n    32→\n    33→| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n    34→|------|---------|----------------|\n    35→| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n    36→| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n    37→| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n    38→| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n    39→| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n    40→| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n    41→| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n    42→| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n    43→| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n    44→| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n    45→| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n    46→| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n    47→| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n    48→| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n    49→\n    50→These file definitions inform the pipeline specification produced in Phase 2.\n    51→\n    52→## Required Input\n    53→\n    54→```\n    55→/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n    56→```\n    57→\n    58→| Argument | Required | Description |\n    59→|----------|----------|-------------|\n    60→| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n    61→| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n    62→| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n    63→| `--dry-run` | No | Execute Phases 0-2 only — emit Phase 0-2 contracts only. No spec synthesis (Phase 3) or panel review (Phase 4) artifacts are produced. |\n    64→\n    65→**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n    66→\n    67→## Behavioral Flow\n    68→\n    69→Four phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n    70→\n    71→### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n    72→\n    73→Load `refs/analysis-protocol.md` before this phase. It contains the discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, and output format template.\n    74→\n    75→**Goal**: Decompose the target workflow into a structured pipeline specification.\n    76→\n    77→1. **Discover components** — Find the command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all `refs/` and `rules/`, all `templates/`, all `scripts/`, and all referenced agents. Build a component inventory table with paths, line counts, and purposes.\n    78→\n    79→2. **Map the protocol** — Extract the step-by-step behavioral flow. Pay attention to:\n    80→   - Wave/phase boundaries in the skill\n    81→   - Agent delegation patterns (which agent, parallel vs sequential)\n    82→   - Conditional execution paths (flags that skip steps)\n    83→   - Implicit inter-step data flow (what one step produces that another consumes)\n    84→\n    85→3. **Identify steps** — A new Step boundary exists when:\n    86→   - A new artifact is produced\n    87→   - A different agent takes over\n    88→   - Execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n    89→   - A quality gate must be evaluated\n    90→   - Operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n    91→\n    92→4. **Classify each step** on the programmatic spectrum:\n    93→   - **Pure programmatic**: Runs as a Python function, no Claude subprocess. Examples: file inventory via `git ls-files`, config construction, file filtering between passes, scoring formulas, structural validation, batch assignment. If the logic is deterministic and the input/output formats are well-defined, it should be programmatic.\n    94→   - **Claude-assisted**: Runs as a Claude subprocess with a prompt contract and gate. Examples: content analysis, writing reports, rubric-based assessment, creative synthesis, debate.\n    95→   - **Hybrid**: Programmatic setup → Claude subprocess → programmatic validation. Examples: steps that need Claude for content but have strict structural validation on the output.\n    96→\n    97→5. **Map dependencies and parallel groups** — Draw the data flow: which steps produce artifacts consumed by downstream steps? Which steps are independent and can run concurrently? For batched workflows, identify where batch parallelism applies.\n    98→\n    99→6. **Extract gates** — For each step output, identify what validation the original workflow expects. Map these to gate tiers: EXEMPT (informational), LIGHT (exists + non-empty), STANDARD (structural checks), STRICT (structural + semantic).\n   100→\n   101→7. **Assign gate modes** — `GateMode.BLOCKING` for steps whose output feeds downstream steps. `GateMode.TRAILING` for quality-only checks that don't affect data flow.\n   102→\n   103→**Output**: `portify-analysis.md` — Complete decomposition following the template in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. Must include: component inventory, step graph with classifications, parallel groups, gates summary, data flow diagram, and recommendations.\n   104→\n   105→**Present to user for review before Phase 2.**\n   106→\n   107→### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n   108→\n   109→Load `refs/pipeline-spec.md` before this phase. It contains Step definition patterns, model design patterns, gate patterns, executor patterns, and the new unified-audit-gating patterns (TurnLedger, gate modes, subprocess isolation, context injection, resume semantics).\n   110→\n   111→**Goal**: Convert the analysis into concrete, code-ready specifications.\n   112→\n   113→1. **Design the Step graph** — Map each workflow step to `Step` objects. Batched parallel steps are `list[Step]` groups. Dynamic step counts (e.g., batch count determined at runtime) require a `build_steps()` function.\n   114→\n   115→2. **Define models** — Design domain-specific dataclasses:\n   116→   - Config extending `PipelineConfig` with workflow-specific fields (source paths, pass selection, batch sizes, stall/timeout policies)\n   117→   - Status enum with domain-specific states (e.g., `VALIDATION_FAIL`, `CRITICAL_FAIL` for audit workflows)\n   118→   - Result extending `StepResult` with execution telemetry\n   119→   - Aggregate result with `resume_command()` and `suggested_resume_budget`\n   120→   - Monitor state with domain-specific NDJSON signals\n   121→   - `TurnLedger` integration for budget tracking\n   122→\n   123→3. **Design prompts** — For each Claude-assisted step, write the prompt builder. Each prompt must specify:\n   124→   - What input files/context to embed (inline for <50KB, `--file` args for larger)\n   125→   - Required output sections and frontmatter fields\n   126→   - Machine-readable markers: `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n   127→   - Any structural requirements the gate will check\n   128→\n   129→   If prompts collectively exceed ~300 lines, put them in a separate `portify-prompts.md`.\n   130→\n   131→4. **Design gates** — For each output artifact, define:\n   132→   - `GateCriteria` with tier, required frontmatter, min lines, semantic checks\n   133→   - `GateMode` (BLOCKING or TRAILING)\n   134→   - All semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]`\n   135→\n   136→5. **Implement pure-programmatic steps** — Write actual runnable Python code for steps classified as \"pure programmatic\". These run as direct function calls in the executor, bypassing `ClaudeProcess`. Include: file discovery, domain classification, batch assignment, inter-pass filtering, config construction.\n   137→\n   138→6. **Design the executor** — Sprint-style synchronous supervisor:\n   139→   - `ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism (NOT async/await)\n   140→   - `time.sleep()` polling loops for monitoring\n   141→   - Per-step/batch monitoring with stall detection\n   142→   - `TurnLedger` budget guards before each launch\n   143→   - Runner-authored truth: reports from observed data, not Claude self-reporting\n   144→   - Subprocess isolation (4-layer model) for child Claude sessions\n   145→   - Context injection: prior results compressed into subsequent prompts\n   146→   - Signal-aware shutdown with `SignalHandler`\n   147→\n   148→7. **Plan integration** — Click command group, main.py import, file generation order.\n   149→\n   150→**Output**: `portify-spec.md` (+ optional `portify-prompts.md`) — Complete code spec with models, gates, executor design, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan.\n   151→\n   152→**Present to user for approval before Phase 3.**\n   153→\n   154→### Phase 3: Release Spec Synthesis\n   155→\n   156→**Phase 2→3 Entry Gate**: Before beginning Phase 3, verify ALL of the following:\n   157→- Phase 2 contract `status: completed`\n   158→- All blocking checks passed (no unresolved BLOCKING gate failures)\n   159→- Phase 2 `step_mapping` contains ≥1 entry\n   160→\n   161→**STOP** if any entry gate condition fails. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n\n- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n\nThe existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n\n## What Gets Generated\n\nA CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n\n| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n|------|---------|----------------|\n| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n\nThese file definitions inform the pipeline specification produced in Phase 2.\n\n## Required Input\n\n```\n/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n```\n\n| Argument | Required | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n| `--dry-run` | No | Execute Phases 0-2 only — emit Phase 0-2 contracts only. No spec synthesis (Phase 3) or panel review (Phase 4) artifacts are produced. |\n\n**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\nFour phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n\n### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n\nLoad `refs/analysis-protocol.md` before this phase. It contains the discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, and output format template.\n\n**Goal**: Decompose the target workflow into a structured pipeline specification.\n\n1. **Discover components** — Find the command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all `refs/` and `rules/`, all `templates/`, all `scripts/`, and all referenced agents. Build a component inventory table with paths, line counts, and purposes.\n\n2. **Map the protocol** — Extract the step-by-step behavioral flow. Pay attention to:\n   - Wave/phase boundaries in the skill\n   - Agent delegation patterns (which agent, parallel vs sequential)\n   - Conditional execution paths (flags that skip steps)\n   - Implicit inter-step data flow (what one step produces that another consumes)\n\n3. **Identify steps** — A new Step boundary exists when:\n   - A new artifact is produced\n   - A different agent takes over\n   - Execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n   - A quality gate must be evaluated\n   - Operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n\n4. **Classify each step** on the programmatic spectrum:\n   - **Pure programmatic**: Runs as a Python function, no Claude subprocess. Examples: file inventory via `git ls-files`, config construction, file filtering between passes, scoring formulas, structural validation, batch assignment. If the logic is deterministic and the input/output formats are well-defined, it should be programmatic.\n   - **Claude-assisted**: Runs as a Claude subprocess with a prompt contract and gate. Examples: content analysis, writing reports, rubric-based assessment, creative synthesis, debate.\n   - **Hybrid**: Programmatic setup → Claude subprocess → programmatic validation. Examples: steps that need Claude for content but have strict structural validation on the output.\n\n5. **Map dependencies and parallel groups** — Draw the data flow: which steps produce artifacts consumed by downstream steps? Which steps are independent and can run concurrently? For batched workflows, identify where batch parallelism applies.\n\n6. **Extract gates** — For each step output, identify what validation the original workflow expects. Map these to gate tiers: EXEMPT (informational), LIGHT (exists + non-empty), STANDARD (structural checks), STRICT (structural + semantic).\n\n7. **Assign gate modes** — `GateMode.BLOCKING` for steps whose output feeds downstream steps. `GateMode.TRAILING` for quality-only checks that don't affect data flow.\n\n**Output**: `portify-analysis.md` — Complete decomposition following the template in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. Must include: component inventory, step graph with classifications, parallel groups, gates summary, data flow diagram, and recommendations.\n\n**Present to user for review before Phase 2.**\n\n### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n\nLoad `refs/pipeline-spec.md` before this phase. It contains Step definition patterns, model design patterns, gate patterns, executor patterns, and the new unified-audit-gating patterns (TurnLedger, gate modes, subprocess isolation, context injection, resume semantics).\n\n**Goal**: Convert the analysis into concrete, code-ready specifications.\n\n1. **Design the Step graph** — Map each workflow step to `Step` objects. Batched parallel steps are `list[Step]` groups. Dynamic step counts (e.g., batch count determined at runtime) require a `build_steps()` function.\n\n2. **Define models** — Design domain-specific dataclasses:\n   - Config extending `PipelineConfig` with workflow-specific fields (source paths, pass selection, batch sizes, stall/timeout policies)\n   - Status enum with domain-specific states (e.g., `VALIDATION_FAIL`, `CRITICAL_FAIL` for audit workflows)\n   - Result extending `StepResult` with execution telemetry\n   - Aggregate result with `resume_command()` and `suggested_resume_budget`\n   - Monitor state with domain-specific NDJSON signals\n   - `TurnLedger` integration for budget tracking\n\n3. **Design prompts** — For each Claude-assisted step, write the prompt builder. Each prompt must specify:\n   - What input files/context to embed (inline for <50KB, `--file` args for larger)\n   - Required output sections and frontmatter fields\n   - Machine-readable markers: `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n   - Any structural requirements the gate will check\n\n   If prompts collectively exceed ~300 lines, put them in a separate `portify-prompts.md`.\n\n4. **Design gates** — For each output artifact, define:\n   - `GateCriteria` with tier, required frontmatter, min lines, semantic checks\n   - `GateMode` (BLOCKING or TRAILING)\n   - All semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]`\n\n5. **Implement pure-programmatic steps** — Write actual runnable Python code for steps classified as \"pure programmatic\". These run as direct function calls in the executor, bypassing `ClaudeProcess`. Include: file discovery, domain classification, batch assignment, inter-pass filtering, config construction.\n\n6. **Design the executor** — Sprint-style synchronous supervisor:\n   - `ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism (NOT async/await)\n   - `time.sleep()` polling loops for monitoring\n   - Per-step/batch monitoring with stall detection\n   - `TurnLedger` budget guards before each launch\n   - Runner-authored truth: reports from observed data, not Claude self-reporting\n   - Subprocess isolation (4-layer model) for child Claude sessions\n   - Context injection: prior results compressed into subsequent prompts\n   - Signal-aware shutdown with `SignalHandler`\n\n7. **Plan integration** — Click command group, main.py import, file generation order.\n\n**Output**: `portify-spec.md` (+ optional `portify-prompts.md`) — Complete code spec with models, gates, executor design, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan.\n\n**Present to user for approval before Phase 3.**\n\n### Phase 3: Release Spec Synthesis\n\n**Phase 2→3 Entry Gate**: Before beginning Phase 3, verify ALL of the following:\n- Phase 2 contract `status: completed`\n- All blocking checks passed (no unresolved BLOCKING gate failures)\n- Phase 2 `step_mapping` contains ≥1 entry\n\n**STOP** if any entry gate condition fails. Report which condition failed and emit the return contract with `failure_phase: 3, failure_type: prerequisite_failed`.\n\n**Goal**: Generate a complete, reviewed release specification from Phase 1 and Phase 2 outputs. Record the wall clock start time for `phase_3_seconds` timing instrumentation.\n\n**Timing**: Record `phase_3_start = current_time()` at the beginning of this phase. NFR-001 advisory target: Phase 3 should complete within 10 minutes wall clock (non-blocking; exceeding this target emits a warning but does not halt the pipeline).\n\n#### Step 3a: Template Instantiation\n\n1. Load the release spec template from `src/superclaude/examples/release-spec-template.md`\n2. Create a working copy at `{work_dir}/portify-release-spec.md`\n3. Verify the working copy was created successfully and contains the template content\n\n#### Step 3b: Content Population\n\nFill template sections from Phase 1 (workflow analysis) and Phase 2 (pipeline specification) outputs using the following mapping table. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DkRGvLK8S3kHPRyQZWCpsU","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Roadmap gate criteria -- data definitions for each pipeline step's gate.\n     2→\n     3→This module defines GateCriteria instances as module-level constants.\n     4→Gate criteria are pure data -- no logic, no imports from pipeline/gates.py\n     5→enforcement code (NFR-005).\n     6→\n     7→Semantic check functions are defined here as pure functions accepting\n     8→file content and returning bool. They are registered on the STRICT-tier\n     9→GateCriteria instances.\n    10→\"\"\"\n    11→\n    12→from __future__ import annotations\n    13→\n    14→from ..pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n    15→\n    16→\n    17→# --- Semantic check functions (pure: content -> bool) ---\n    18→\n    19→def _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    20→    \"\"\"Verify heading levels increment by at most 1 (no H2 -> H4 skip).\"\"\"\n    21→    prev_level = 0\n    22→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    23→        stripped = line.lstrip()\n    24→        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n    25→            level = 0\n    26→            for ch in stripped:\n    27→                if ch == \"#\":\n    28→                    level += 1\n    29→                else:\n    30→                    break\n    31→            if prev_level > 0 and level > prev_level + 1:\n    32→                return False\n    33→            prev_level = level\n    34→    return True\n    35→\n    36→\n    37→def _cross_refs_resolve(content: str) -> bool:\n    38→    \"\"\"Verify all 'See section' / cross-reference patterns have matching headings.\n    39→\n    40→    Checks that internal references like 'See section X.Y' or 'See X.Y'\n    41→    have corresponding headings. Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    42→    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    43→    \"\"\"\n    44→    import re\n    45→    import warnings\n    46→\n    47→    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    48→    headings: set[str] = set()\n    49→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    50→        stripped = line.lstrip()\n    51→        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n    52→            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n    53→            headings.add(heading_text)\n    54→\n    55→    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    56→    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    57→    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    58→    if not refs:\n    59→        return True\n    60→\n    61→    # Check each reference against headings\n    62→    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    63→    for ref in refs:\n    64→        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n    65→        if not found:\n    66→            # Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    if not refs:\n        return True\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - high_severity_count field is missing\n    - high_severity_count > 0\n\n    Raises TypeError if high_severity_count value is not parseable as an integer.\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    value = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if value is None:\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        count = int(value)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        raise TypeError(\n            f\"high_severity_count must be an integer, got: {value!r}\"\n        )\n\n    return count == 0\n\n\ndef _has_per_finding_table(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify the certification report contains a per-finding results table.\n\n    Checks for a markdown table with the required columns:\n    Finding | Severity | Result | Justification\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    # Look for the table header row with required columns\n    has_header = bool(re.search(\n        r\"\\|\\s*Finding\\s*\\|\\s*Severity\\s*\\|\\s*Result\\s*\\|\\s*Justification\\s*\\|\",\n        content,\n        re.IGNORECASE,\n    ))\n    # Also check for at least one data row (| F-XX | ... |)\n    has_data = bool(re.search(\n        r\"\\|\\s*F-\\d+\\s*\\|\",\n        content,\n    ))\n    return has_header and has_data\n\n\ndef _all_actionable_have_status(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all non-SKIPPED entries in remediation tasklist have FIXED or FAILED status.\n\n    Scans for checklist entries matching `- [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS -- desc`\n    and ensures STATUS is one of FIXED or FAILED (not PENDING).\n    SKIPPED entries (marked `- [x]`) are excluded from the check.\n\n    Returns True if all actionable entries have terminal status, or if there\n    are no actionable entries. Returns False if any PENDING entry is found.\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n\n    # Match actionable (unchecked) entries: - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS -- desc\n    actionable_pattern = re.compile(\n        r\"^-\\s+\\[ \\]\\s+F-\\d+\\s*\\|[^|]*\\|\\s*(\\w+)\\s*--\",\n        re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    for match in actionable_pattern.finditer(content):\n        status = match.group(1).upper()\n        if status not in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\"):\n            return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _tasklist_ready_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate tasklist_ready is consistent with severity counts.\n\n    Consistency rule: tasklist_ready=true requires high_severity_count=0\n    AND validation_complete=true. If tasklist_ready=true but either\n    condition fails, this returns False (inconsistency detected).\n\n    Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - tasklist_ready field is missing\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but high_severity_count > 0\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but validation_complete != true\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm \"true\"\n\n    if not tasklist_ready:\n        # If tasklist_ready is false, that's always consistent\n        return True\n\n    # tasklist_ready is true -- verify consistency\n    high_str = 56→\n    57→def _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    58→    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n    59→\n    60→    Each file is wrapped in a fenced Uses atomic write (write to ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent\n    78→    partial file states.\n    79→\n    80→    Returns the byte count of the stripped preamble (0 when file already starts\n    81→    with ``---`` or no frontmatter delimiter is found).\n    82→    \"\"\"\n    83→    import os\n    84→    import re\n    85→\n    86→    try:\n    87→        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    88→    except FileNotFoundError:\n    89→        return 0\n    90→\n    91→    # 304→\n   305→    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n   306→    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n   307→    \"\"\"\n   308→    out = config.output_dir\n   309→\n   310→    # Agent specs\n   311→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   312→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   313→\n   314→    # Output paths\n   315→    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n   316→    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n   317→    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n   318→    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n   319→    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n   320→    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n   321→    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n   322→    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n   323→    spec_fidelity_file = out / \"spec-fidelity.md\"\n   324→\n   325→    # Load retrospective content if configured (missing file handled gracefully)\n   326→    retrospective_content: str | None = None\n   327→    if config.retrospective_file is not None and config.retrospective_file.is_file():\n   328→        try:\n   329→            retrospective_content = config.retrospective_file.read_text(\n   330→                encoding=\"utf-8\"\n   331→            )\n   332→        except OSError:\n   333→            retrospective_content = None\n   334→\n   335→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]] = [\n   336→        # Step 1: Extract\n   337→        Step(\n   338→            id=\"extract\",\n   339→            prompt=build_extract_prompt(\n   340→                config.spec_file,\n   341→                retrospective_content=retrospective_content,\n   342→            ),\n   343→            output_file=extraction,\n   344→            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n   345→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   346→            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n   347→            retry_limit=1,\n   348→        ),\n   349→        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n   350→        [\n   351→            Step(\n   352→                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   353→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n   354→                output_file=roadmap_a,\n   355→                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n   356→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   357→                inputs=[extraction],\n   358→                retry_limit=1,\n   359→                model=agent_a.model,\n   360→            ),\n   361→            Step(\n   362→                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   363→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_b, extraction),\n   364→                output_file=roadmap_b,\n   365→                gate=GENERATE_B_GATE,\n   366→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   367→                inputs=[extraction],\n   368→                retry_limit=1,\n   369→                model=agent_b.model,\n   370→            ),\n   371→        ],\n   372→        # Step 3: Diff\n   373→        Step(\n   374→            id=\"diff\",\n   375→            prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   376→            output_file=diff_file,\n   377→            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n   378→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   379→            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   380→            retry_limit=1,\n   381→        ),\n   382→        # Step 4: Debate\n   383→        Step(\n   384→            id=\"debate\",\n   385→            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n   386→            output_file=debate_file,\n   387→            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n   388→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   389→            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   390→            retry_limit=1,\n   391→        ),\n   392→        # Step 5: Score\n   393→        Step(\n   394→            id=\"score\",\n   395→            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   396→            output_file=score_file,\n   397→            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n   398→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   399→            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   400→            retry_limit=1,\n   401→        ),\n   402→        # Step 6: Merge\n   403→        Step(\n   404→            id=\"merge\",\n   405→            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n   406→            output_file=merge_file,\n   407→            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n   408→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   409→            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n   410→            retry_limit=1,\n   411→        ),\n   412→        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n   413→        Step(\n   414→            id=\"test-strategy\",\n   415→            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n   416→            output_file=test_strat,\n   417→            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n   418→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   419→            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n   420→            retry_limit=1,\n   421→        ),\n   422→        # Step 8: Spec Fidelity (after test-strategy, FR-008 through FR-010)\n   423→        Step(\n   424→            id=\"spec-fidelity\",\n   425→            prompt=build_spec_fidelity_prompt(config.spec_file, merge_file),\n   426→            output_file=spec_fidelity_file,\n   427→            gate=SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n   428→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   429→            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n   430→            retry_limit=1,\n   431→        ),\n   432→    ]\n   433→\n   434→    return steps\n   435→\n   436→\n   437→def _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n   438→    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n   439→    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   440→    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n   441→    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n   442→\n   443→    if not failed:\n   444→        return \"\"\n   445→\n   446→    fail = failed[-1]\n   447→    step = fail.step\n   448→\n   449→    # Calculate file details if output exists\n   450→    file_info = \"\"\n   451→    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n   452→        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   453→        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   454→        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n   455→        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n   456→    elif step:\n   457→        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n   458→\n   459→    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   460→\n   461→    lines = [\n   462→        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n   463→        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n   464→        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n   465→        file_info.rstrip(),\n   466→        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n   467→        \"\",\n   468→        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n   469→        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n   470→    ]\n   471→\n   472→    # Collect skipped steps\n   473→    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n   474→    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n   475→    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n   476→    if cancelled:\n   477→        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n   478→\n   479→    if skipped_ids:\n   480→        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n   481→\n   482→    lines.extend([\n   483→        \"\",\n   484→        \"To retry from this step:\",\n   485→        f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n   486→        \"\",\n   487→        \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n   488→        f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n   489→    ])\n   490→\n   491→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   492→\n   493→\n   494→def _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n   495→    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n   496→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   497→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   498→    return [\n   499→        \"extract\",\n   500→        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   501→        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   502→        \"diff\",\n   503→        \"debate\",\n   504→        \"score\",\n   505→        \"merge\",\n   506→        \"test-strategy\",\n   507→        \"spec-fidelity\",\n   508→        \"remediate\",\n   509→        \"certify\",\n   510→    ]\n   511→\n   512→\n   513→def _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n   514→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n   515→    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n   516→\n   517→\n   518→def _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n   519→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n   520→    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   521→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   522→        print(\n   523→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   524→            flush=True,\n   525→        )\n   526→    else:\n   527→        print(\n   528→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   529→            flush=True,\n   530→        )\n   531→        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n   532→            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n   533→\n   534→\n   535→def _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n   536→    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n   537→    step_num = 0\n   538→    for entry in steps:\n   539→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   540→            for s in entry:\n   541→                step_num += 1\n   542→                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n   543→        else:\n   544→            step_num += 1\n   545→            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n   546→\n   547→\n   548→def _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n   549→    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n   550→    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n   551→    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n   552→    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n   553→    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n   554→    if step.model:\n   555→        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n   556→    if step.gate:\n   557→        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n   558→        print(f\"  Gate min_lines: {step.gate.min_lines}\")\n   559→        if step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n   560→            print(f\"  Gate frontmatter: {', '.join(step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields)}\")\n   561→        if step.gate.semantic_checks:\n   562→            checks = [c.name for c in step.gate.semantic_checks]\n   563→            print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n   564→    print()\n   565→\n   566→\n   567→def _save_state(\n   568→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   569→    results: list[StepResult],\n   570→    remediate_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   571→    certify_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   572→) -> None:\n   573→    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n   574→\n   575→    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n   576→    and ``certify`` keys if present. Accepts optional remediate/certify\n   577→    metadata dicts for Phase 6 state finalization.\n   578→    \"\"\"\n   579→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   580→    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   581→\n   582→    # Preserve existing keys across state rewrites\n   583→    existing = read_state(state_file)\n   584→    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n   585→    existing_fidelity = existing.get(\"fidelity_status\") if existing else None\n   586→    existing_remediate = existing.get(\"remediate\") if existing else None\n   587→    existing_certify = existing.get(\"certify\") if existing else None\n   588→\n   589→    state = {\n   590→        \"schema_version\": 1,\n   591→        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n   592→        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n   593→        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n   594→        \"depth\": config.depth,\n   595→        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   596→        \"steps\": {\n   597→            r.step.id: {\n   598→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   599→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   600→                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n   601→                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n   602→                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n   603→            }\n   604→            for r in results\n   605→            if r.step\n   606→        },\n   607→    }\n   608→\n   609→    if existing_validation is not None:\n   610→        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n   611→\n   612→    # Derive fidelity_status from spec-fidelity step result\n   613→    fidelity_result = next(\n   614→        (r for r in results if r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"),\n   615→        None,\n   616→    )\n   617→    if fidelity_result is not None:\n   618→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = _derive_fidelity_status(fidelity_result)\n   619→    elif existing_fidelity is not None:\n   620→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = existing_fidelity\n   621→\n   622→    # Remediate metadata (spec §3.1)\n   623→    if remediate_metadata is not None:\n   624→        state[\"remediate\"] = remediate_metadata\n   625→    elif existing_remediate is not None:\n   626→        state[\"remediate\"] = existing_remediate\n   627→\n   628→    # Certify metadata (spec §3.1)\n   629→    if certify_metadata is not None:\n   630→        state[\"certify\"] = certify_metadata\n   631→    elif existing_certify is not None:\n   632→        state[\"certify\"] = existing_certify\n   633→\n   634→    write_state(state, state_file)\n   635→\n   636→\n   637→def _derive_fidelity_status(result: StepResult) -> str:\n   638→    \"\"\"Derive fidelity_status enum from a spec-fidelity StepResult.\n   639→\n   640→    Returns one of: 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'degraded'.\n   641→    \"\"\"\n   642→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   643→        # Check if the output indicates degraded mode\n   644→        if result.step and result.step.output_file.exists():\n   645→            content = result.step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   646→            if \"validation_complete: false\" in content:\n   647→                return \"degraded\"\n   648→        return \"pass\"\n   649→    if result.status == StepStatus.SKIPPED:\n   650→        return \"skipped\"\n   651→    if result.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT):\n   652→        return \"fail\"\n   653→    return \"skipped\"\n   654→\n   655→\n   656→def generate_degraded_report(\n   657→    output_file: Path,\n   658→    failed_agent: str,\n   659→    failure_reason: str,\n   660→) -> None:\n   661→    \"\"\"Generate a degraded fidelity report when the agent fails.\n   662→\n   663→    Produces a report with validation_complete=false and\n   664→    fidelity_check_attempted=true so the SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE\n   665→    can distinguish degraded from clean passes (NFR-007).\n   666→\n   667→    The degraded report names the failed agent and reason in the body.\n   668→    \"\"\"\n   669→    report = (\n   670→        \"---\\n\"\n   671→        \"high_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n   672→        \"medium_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n   673→        \"low_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n   674→        \"total_deviations: 0\\n\"\n   675→        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n   676→        \"fidelity_check_attempted: true\\n\"\n   677→        \"tasklist_ready: false\\n\"\n   678→        \"---\\n\"\n   679→        \"\\n\"\n   680→        \"## Degraded Fidelity Report\\n\"\n   681→        \"\\n\"\n   682→        \"Spec-fidelity validation could not be completed.\\n\"\n   683→        \"\\n\"\n   684→        f\"**Failed Agent**: {failed_agent}\\n\"\n   685→        f\"**Failure Reason**: {failure_reason}\\n\"\n   686→        \"\\n\"\n   687→        \"This is a degraded report produced after agent failure and retry \"\n   688→        \"exhaustion. No deviations were analyzed. The pipeline continues \"\n   689→        \"in degraded mode with validation_complete=false.\\n\"\n   690→        \"\\n\"\n   691→        \"### Recommended Actions\\n\"\n   692→        \"\\n\"\n   693→        \"1. Investigate the agent failure\\n\"\n   694→        \"2. Re-run the spec-fidelity step manually\\n\"\n   695→        \"3. Review the roadmap against the specification\\n\"\n   696→    )\n   697→    output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   698→    output_file.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   699→\n   700→\n   701→def build_remediate_metadata(\n   702→    status: str,\n   703→    scope: str,\n   704→    findings_total: int,\n   705→    findings_actionable: int,\n   706→    findings_fixed: int,\n   707→    findings_failed: int,\n   708→    findings_skipped: int,\n   709→    agents_spawned: int,\n   710→    tasklist_file: str,\n   711→) -> dict:\n   712→    \"\"\"Build remediate metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n   713→\n   714→    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``remediate`` entry fields.\n   715→    \"\"\"\n   716→    return {\n   717→        \"status\": status,\n   718→        \"scope\": scope,\n   719→        \"findings_total\": findings_total,\n   720→        \"findings_actionable\": findings_actionable,\n   721→        \"findings_fixed\": findings_fixed,\n   722→        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n   723→        \"findings_skipped\": findings_skipped,\n   724→        \"agents_spawned\": agents_spawned,\n   725→        \"tasklist_file\": tasklist_file,\n   726→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   727→    }\n   728→\n   729→\n   730→def build_certify_metadata(\n   731→    status: str,\n   732→    findings_verified: int,\n   733→    findings_passed: int,\n   734→    findings_failed: int,\n   735→    certified: bool,\n   736→    report_file: str,\n   737→) -> dict:\n   738→    \"\"\"Build certify metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n   739→\n   740→    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``certify`` entry fields.\n   741→    \"\"\"\n   742→    return {\n   743→        \"status\": status,\n   744→        \"findings_verified\": findings_verified,\n   745→        \"findings_passed\": findings_passed,\n   746→        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n   747→        \"certified\": certified,\n   748→        \"report_file\": report_file,\n   749→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   750→    }\n   751→\n   752→\n   753→def derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n   754→    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n   755→\n   756→    State transitions at each boundary:\n   757→    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n   758→    - post-remediate: remediated\n   759→    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n   760→\n   761→    Returns one of: 'pending', 'validated', 'validated-with-issues',\n   762→    'remediated', 'certified', 'certified-with-caveats'.\n   763→    \"\"\"\n   764→    certify = state.get(\"certify\")\n   765→    if certify is not None:\n   766→        if certify.get(\"certified\", False):\n   767→            return \"certified\"\n   768→        return \"certified-with-caveats\"\n   769→\n   770→    remediate = state.get(\"remediate\")\n   771→    if remediate is not None:\n   772→        return \"remediated\"\n   773→\n   774→    validation = state.get(\"validation\")\n   775→    if validation is not None:\n   776→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"pass\":\n   777→            return \"validated\"\n   778→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"fail\":\n   779→            return \"validated-with-issues\"\n   780→\n   781→    return \"pending\"\n   782→\n   783→\n   784→def write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n   785→    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n   786→    import os as _os\n   787→\n   788→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   789→    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n   790→    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   791→    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n   792→\n   793→\n   794→def read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n   795→    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n   796→    if not path.exists():\n   797→        return None\n   798→    try:\n   799→        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   800→        if not text.strip():\n   801→            return None\n   802→        return json.loads(text)\n   803→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n   804→        return None\n   805→\n   806→\n   807→def apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n   808→    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n   809→\n   810→    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n   811→    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n   812→\n   813→    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n   814→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n   815→\n   816→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n   817→    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n   818→    \"\"\"\n   819→    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n   820→\n   821→\n   822→def execute_roadmap(config: RoadmapConfig, resume: bool = False, no_validate: bool = False) -> None:\n   823→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n   824→\n   825→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n   826→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n   827→\n   828→    After all 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n   829→    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n   830→\n   831→    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n   832→    (FR-010, AC-005). It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n   833→    subsystem.\n   834→    \"\"\"\n   835→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   836→\n   837→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n   838→\n   839→    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n   840→    if config.dry_run:\n   841→        _dry_run_output(steps)\n   842→        return\n   843→\n   844→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n   845→    if resume:\n   846→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n   847→\n   848→        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n   849→\n   850→    # Execute pipeline\n   851→    results = execute_pipeline(\n   852→        steps=steps,\n   853→        config=config,\n   854→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n   855→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n   856→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n   857→    )\n   858→\n   859→    # Save state\n   860→    _save_state(config, results)\n   861→\n   862→    # Check for failures\n   863→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   864→    if failures:\n   865→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, 100 KB\n\n\ndef _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n\n    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n\n\ndef _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble before YAML frontmatter in a step output file.\n\n    Reads the file, finds the first ``^---`` line, and removes everything before\n    it.  Uses atomic write (write to ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent\n    partial file states.\n\n    Returns the byte count of the stripped preamble (0 when file already starts\n    with ``---`` or no frontmatter delimiter is found).\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- nothing to strip\n    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n        return 0\n\n    # Search for the first ^--- line\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start() :]\n    preamble_bytes = len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n    output_file: Path,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n\n    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    diagnostics_line = (\n        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n        f\"started_at: \\\"{started_at.isoformat()}\\\", \"\n        f\"finished_at: \\\"{finished_at.isoformat()}\\\"}}\"\n    )\n\n    # Insert before the closing ---\n    new_content = (\n        content[: end_idx]\n        + \"\\n\"\n        + diagnostics_line\n        + content[end_idx:]\n    )\n    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef roadmap_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n\n    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n    \"\"\"\n    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n            attempt=1,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n    if embedded and len(embedded.encode(\"utf-8\")) <= _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n        extra_args: list[str] = []\n    elif embedded:\n        _log.warning(\n            \"Step '%s': embedded inputs exceed %d bytes, falling back to --file flags\",\n            step.id,\n            _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n        )\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = [\n            arg\n            for input_path in step.inputs\n            for arg in (\"--file\", str(input_path))\n        ]\n    else:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = []\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=effective_prompt,\n        output_file=step.output_file,\n        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=1,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n\n    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef build_certify_step(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    findings: list | None = None,\n    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n) -> Step:\n    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n\n    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n    per spec section 2.5. \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n\n    if not failed:\n        return \"\"\n\n    fail = failed[-1]\n    step = fail.step\n\n    # Calculate    f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n        file_info.rstrip(),\n        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n    ]\n\n    # Collect skipped steps\n    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n    skipped_ids = steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n\n    lines.extend([\n        \"\",\n        \"To retry from this step:\",\n        f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n        \"\",\n        \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n        f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n    ])\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n    return [\n        \"extract\",\n \"spec-fidelity\",\n        \"remediate\",\n        \"certify\",\n    ]\n\n\ndef _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n       flush=True,\n        )\n    else:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n    step_num = 0\n    for entry in steps:\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            for s in entry:\n                step_num += None,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n\n    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n    and ``certify`` Review the roadmap against the specification\\n\"\n    )\n    output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    output_file.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n\n    State transitions at each boundary:\n    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n    - post-remediate: remediated\n    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n\n    Returns one of: 'pending', 'validated', 'validated-with-issues',\n    'remediated', 'certified', 'certified-with-caveats'.\n    \"\"\"\n    certify = state.get(\"certify\")\n    if certify is not None:\n        if certify.get(\"certified\", False):\n            return         return \"validated-with-issues\"\n\n    return \"pending\"\n\n\ndef write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n\n    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n    (FR-010, AC-005). Passes REMEDIATE_GATE\n    3. source_report_hash matches current validation report SHA-256\n\n    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .gates import REMEDIATE_GATE\n\n    tasklist_file = config.output_dir / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"\n    if not tasklist_file.exists():\n        return False\n\n    passed, _reason = gate_fn(tasklist_file, REMEDIATE_GATE)\n    if not passed:\n        return False\n\n    # Hash check: verify tasklist was generated from current validation report\n    if not _check_tasklist_hash_current(tasklist_file, config.output_dir):\n        print(\n            \"[roadmap] Remediation tasklist stale (hash mismatch), will re-run remediate\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef check_certify_resume(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    gate_fn: Callable,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the certify step can be skipped on --resume.\n\n    Returns True (skip) when:\n    1. certification-report.md exists\n    2. Returns False on mismatch\n    (fail closed).\n    \"\"\"\n    from .gates import _parse_frontmatter\n\n    content = tasklist_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    saved_hash = fm.get(\"source_report_hash\", \"\")\n    if not saved_hash:\n        return False\n\n    # Find the validation report (source_report field)\n    source_report = fm.get(\"source_report\", \"\")\n    if source_report:\n        report_path = Path(source_report)\n        if not report_path.is_absolute():\n            report_path = output_dir / report_path\n    else:\n        # Default to reflect-merged.md or merged-validation-report.md\n        report_path = output_dir / \"reflect-merged.md\"\n        if not report_path.exists():\n            report_path = output_dir / \"merged-validation-report.md\"\n\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        return False\n\n    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(report_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n    return saved_hash == current_hash\n\n\ndef _apply_resume(\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    gate_fn: Callable,\n) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs already pass gates.\n\n    Also checks for stale spec detection.\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n    force_extract = False\n    if state is not None:\n        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n            print(\n                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n                file=sys.stderr,\n                flush=True,\n            )\n            force_extract = True\n\n    skipped = 0\n    result: list[Step | list[Step]] = []\n    found_failure = False\n\n    for entry in steps:\n        if found_failure:\n            # After first failing step, include all remaining steps\n            result.append(entry)\n            continue\n\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            # Parallel group: check all steps\n            all_pass = True\n            for s in entry:\n                if s.gate:\n                    passed, _reason = gate_fn(s.output_file, s.gate)\n                    if not passed:\n                        all_pass = False\n                        break\n                else:\n                    all_pass = False\n                    break\n            if all_pass:\n                skipped += len(entry)\n                print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {', '.join(s.id for s in entry)} (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n        else:\n            # Force re-run of extract on stale spec\n            if force_extract and entry.id == \"extract\":\n                found_failure = True\n                result.append(entry)\n                continue\n\n            if entry.gate:\n                passed, _reason = gate_fn(entry.output_file, entry.gate)\n                if passed:\n                    skipped += 1\n                    print(f\"[roadmap] Skipping {entry.id} (gate passes)\", flush=True)\n                    continue\n            found_failure = True\n            result.append(entry)\n\n    if skipped > 0:\n        print(f\"[roadmap] Skipped {skipped} steps (gates pass)\", flush=True)\n\n    if not result:\n        print(\"[roadmap] All steps already pass gates. Extend guard boundary analysis to include zero/empty cases — for every guard condition, verify the spec defines behavior for zero, empty, null, and negative inputs.\n   252→\n   253→3. **Whittaker (Adversarial)**: Apply five attack methodologies (Zero/Empty, Divergence, Sentinel Collision, Sequence, Accumulation) against each identified invariant. Produce concrete attack scenarios with state traces demonstrating specification gaps.\n   254→\n   255→4. **Crispin (Testing)**: Analyze testing strategy coverage, acceptance criteria quality, edge case identification, and quality attribute specifications. Generate boundary value test cases for every guard condition and state variable covering: below minimum, at minimum, typical, at maximum, above maximum, and degenerate (zero/empty/null).\n   256→\n   257→**Focus dimensions**: `correctness` and `architecture` — findings must address both dimensions (SC-006).\n   258→\n   259→**Output format**: Each finding uses the structured schema:\n   260→```\n   261→{finding_id, severity(CRITICAL|MAJOR|MINOR), expert, location, issue, recommendation}\n   262→```\n   263→\n   264→Where:\n   265→- `finding_id`: Unique identifier (e.g., `F-001`, `F-002`)\n   266→- `severity`: One of `CRITICAL` (spec provably wrong), `MAJOR` (spec ambiguous/incomplete), `MINOR` (spec could be clearer)\n   267→- `expert`: One of `Fowler`, `Nygard`, `Whittaker`, `Crispin`\n   268→- `location`: Section/requirement reference in the spec\n   269→- `issue`: Description of the identified problem\n   270→- `recommendation`: Specific actionable fix\n   271→\n   272→Additionally produce, if applicable:\n   273→- Guard Condition Boundary Table (Nygard leads, Crispin validates, Whittaker attacks)\n   274→- Pipeline Quantity Flow Diagram (Fowler leads, if pipeline stages detected)\n   275→- State Variable Registry (if 3+ mutable state variables identified)\n   276→\n   277→#### Step 4b: Focus Incorporation\n   278→\n   279→Review focus findings from step 4a. Either:\n   284→  - Incorporate the fix into the relevant spec body section identified by `location`, OR\n   285→  - Document a written justification for dismissal in the panel report (Constraint 7). Dismissal justification must include: `finding_id`, reason for dismissal, and assessment of downstream impact.\n   286→- **MAJOR findings**: Incorporate into the corresponding spec body section identified by `location`. Modifications use append/extend only.\n   287→- **MINOR findings**: Append to Section 11 (Open Items) with the `finding_id`, issue summary, and recommended resolution target.\n   288→\n   289→All modifications are traceable by `finding_id` from step 4a output.
