{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014z8zNP3p7KdPoD8K3PkpWm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→---\n     2→name: sc-cli-portify-protocol\n     3→description: \"Full behavioral protocol for sc:cli-portify — port inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines with sprint-style supervised execution.\"\n     4→category: development\n     5→complexity: high\n     6→allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task\n     7→mcp-servers: [sequential, serena, context7, auggie-mcp]\n     8→personas: [architect, analyzer, backend]\n     9→argument-hint: \"--workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>] [--dry-run]\"\n    10→---\n    11→\n    12→# sc:cli-portify — Workflow-to-CLI Pipeline Compiler\n    13→\n    14→Convert inference-based SuperClaude workflows into programmatic CLI pipelines that ensure 100% completion and consistency between runs.\n    15→\n    16→## Purpose\n    17→\n    18→SuperClaude workflows implemented as commands + skills + agents + refs rely entirely on Claude's reasoning for orchestration. This works well for flexible, one-off tasks but breaks down for repeatable multi-step pipelines where you need:\n    19→\n    20→- **Deterministic control flow** — Python decides what runs when, not inference\n    21→- **Formal artifact validation** — Gates check output quality programmatically\n    22→- **Resume/retry** — Failed steps resume without re-running everything\n    23→- **Live monitoring** — TUI shows progress, stall detection, diagnostics\n    24→- **Budget economics** — Turn ledger tracks consumption across subprocesses\n    25→- **Consistent behavior** — Same inputs produce structurally identical outputs every run\n    26→\n    27→The existing `pipeline/` and `sprint/` modules already solve these problems. This skill bridges the gap by analyzing a workflow and generating a new CLI module that uses these proven patterns.\n    28→\n    29→## What Gets Generated\n    30→\n    31→A CLI subcommand package under `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`:\n    32→\n    33→| File | Purpose | When Generated |\n    34→|------|---------|----------------|\n    35→| `models.py` | Domain types extending `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult` | Always |\n    36→| `gates.py` | `GateCriteria` + semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]` | Always |\n    37→| `prompts.py` | LLM contract builders per Claude-assisted step | Always |\n    38→| `config.py` | CLI arg resolution, file discovery, config construction | Always |\n    39→| `executor.py` | Synchronous supervisor loop with `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism | Always |\n    40→| `monitor.py` | NDJSON incremental output parser with domain signals | Always |\n    41→| `process.py` | `ClaudeProcess` subclass with domain-specific prompt building | Always |\n    42→| `tui.py` | Rich live dashboard with gate state machine | Always |\n    43→| `logging_.py` | Dual JSONL + Markdown execution logging | Always |\n    44→| `diagnostics.py` | `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator` | Always |\n    45→| `commands.py` | Click CLI group and subcommands | Always |\n    46→| `__init__.py` | Package exports | Always |\n    47→| `inventory.py` | Pure-programmatic file discovery/classification | If workflow has discovery steps |\n    48→| `filtering.py` | Pure-programmatic inter-step data filtering | If workflow has filtering between steps |\n    49→\n    50→These file definitions inform the pipeline specification produced in Phase 2.\n    51→\n    52→## Required Input\n    53→\n    54→```\n    55→/sc:cli-portify --workflow <skill-name-or-path> [--name <cli-name>] [--output <dir>]\n    56→```\n    57→\n    58→| Argument | Required | Description |\n    59→|----------|----------|-------------|\n    60→| `--workflow` | Yes | Skill directory path or `sc-*` name to portify |\n    61→| `--name` | No | CLI subcommand name (default: derived from workflow) |\n    62→| `--output` | No | Output directory (default: `src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`) |\n    63→| `--dry-run` | No | Execute Phases 0-2 only — emit Phase 0-2 contracts only. No spec synthesis (Phase 3) or panel review (Phase 4) artifacts are produced. |\n    64→\n    65→**STOP** if `--workflow` is not provided or the path doesn't resolve to a valid skill.\n    66→\n    67→## Behavioral Flow\n    68→\n    69→Four phases, each producing artifacts that feed the next. Present output to user for review after Phases 1 and 2 before continuing.\n    70→\n    71→### Phase 1: Workflow Analysis\n    72→\n    73→Load `refs/analysis-protocol.md` before this phase. It contains the discovery checklist, step decomposition algorithm, classification rubric, and output format template.\n    74→\n    75→**Goal**: Decompose the target workflow into a structured pipeline specification.\n    76→\n    77→1. **Discover components** — Find the command `.md`, skill `SKILL.md`, all `refs/` and `rules/`, all `templates/`, all `scripts/`, and all referenced agents. Build a component inventory table with paths, line counts, and purposes.\n    78→\n    79→2. **Map the protocol** — Extract the step-by-step behavioral flow. Pay attention to:\n    80→   - Wave/phase boundaries in the skill\n    81→   - Agent delegation patterns (which agent, parallel vs sequential)\n    82→   - Conditional execution paths (flags that skip steps)\n    83→   - Implicit inter-step data flow (what one step produces that another consumes)\n    84→\n    85→3. **Identify steps** — A new Step boundary exists when:\n    86→   - A new artifact is produced\n    87→   - A different agent takes over\n    88→   - Execution mode changes (sequential → parallel)\n    89→   - A quality gate must be evaluated\n    90→   - Operation type changes (analysis → generation → validation)\n    91→\n    92→4. **Classify each step** on the programmatic spectrum:\n    93→   - **Pure programmatic**: Runs as a Python function, no Claude subprocess. Examples: file inventory via `git ls-files`, config construction, file filtering between passes, scoring formulas, structural validation, batch assignment. If the logic is deterministic and the input/output formats are well-defined, it should be programmatic.\n    94→   - **Claude-assisted**: Runs as a Claude subprocess with a prompt contract and gate. Examples: content analysis, writing reports, rubric-based assessment, creative synthesis, debate.\n    95→   - **Hybrid**: Programmatic setup → Claude subprocess → programmatic validation. Examples: steps that need Claude for content but have strict structural validation on the output.\n    96→\n    97→5. **Map dependencies and parallel groups** — Draw the data flow: which steps produce artifacts consumed by downstream steps? Which steps are independent and can run concurrently? For batched workflows, identify where batch parallelism applies.\n    98→\n    99→6. **Extract gates** — For each step output, identify what validation the original workflow expects. Map these to gate tiers: EXEMPT (informational), LIGHT (exists + non-empty), STANDARD (structural checks), STRICT (structural + semantic).\n   100→\n   101→7. **Assign gate modes** — `GateMode.BLOCKING` for steps whose output feeds downstream steps. `GateMode.TRAILING` for quality-only checks that don't affect data flow.\n   102→\n   103→**Output**: `portify-analysis.md` — Complete decomposition following the template in `refs/analysis-protocol.md`. Must include: component inventory, step graph with classifications, parallel groups, gates summary, data flow diagram, and recommendations.\n   104→\n   105→**Present to user for review before Phase 2.**\n   106→\n   107→### Phase 2: Pipeline Specification\n   108→\n   109→Load `refs/pipeline-spec.md` before this phase. It contains Step definition patterns, model design patterns, gate patterns, executor patterns, and the new unified-audit-gating patterns (TurnLedger, gate modes, subprocess isolation, context injection, resume semantics).\n   110→\n   111→**Goal**: Convert the analysis into concrete, code-ready specifications.\n   112→\n   113→1. **Design the Step graph** — Map each workflow step to `Step` objects. Batched parallel steps are `list[Step]` groups. Dynamic step counts (e.g., batch count determined at runtime) require a `build_steps()` function.\n   114→\n   115→2. **Define models** — Design domain-specific dataclasses:\n   116→   - Config extending `PipelineConfig` with workflow-specific fields (source paths, pass selection, batch sizes, stall/timeout policies)\n   117→   - Status enum with domain-specific states (e.g., `VALIDATION_FAIL`, `CRITICAL_FAIL` for audit workflows)\n   118→   - Result extending `StepResult` with execution telemetry\n   119→   - Aggregate result with `resume_command()` and `suggested_resume_budget`\n   120→   - Monitor state with domain-specific NDJSON signals\n   121→   - `TurnLedger` integration for budget tracking\n   122→\n   123→3. **Design prompts** — For each Claude-assisted step, write the prompt builder. Each prompt must specify:\n   124→   - What input files/context to embed (inline for <50KB, `--file` args for larger)\n   125→   - Required output sections and frontmatter fields\n   126→   - Machine-readable markers: `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT`\n   127→   - Any structural requirements the gate will check\n   128→\n   129→   If prompts collectively exceed ~300 lines, put them in a separate `portify-prompts.md`.\n   130→\n   131→4. **Design gates** — For each output artifact, define:\n   132→   - `GateCriteria` with tier, required frontmatter, min lines, semantic checks\n   133→   - `GateMode` (BLOCKING or TRAILING)\n   134→   - All semantic check functions returning `tuple[bool, str]`\n   135→\n   136→5. **Implement pure-programmatic steps** — Write actual runnable Python code for steps classified as \"pure programmatic\". These run as direct function calls in the executor, bypassing `ClaudeProcess`. Include: file discovery, domain classification, batch assignment, inter-pass filtering, config construction.\n   137→\n   138→6. **Design the executor** — Sprint-style synchronous supervisor:\n   139→   - `ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism (NOT async/await)\n   140→   - `time.sleep()` polling loops for monitoring\n   141→   - Per-step/batch monitoring with stall detection\n   142→   - `TurnLedger` budget guards before each launch\n   143→   - Runner-authored truth: reports from observed data, not Claude self-reporting\n   144→   - Subprocess isolation (4-layer model) for child Claude sessions\n   145→   - Context injection: prior results compressed into subsequent prompts\n   146→   - Signal-aware shutdown with `SignalHandler`\n   147→\n   148→7. **Plan integration** — Click command group, main.py import, file generation order.\n   149→\n   150→**Output**: `portify-spec.md` (+ optional `portify-prompts.md`) — Complete code spec with models, gates, executor design, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan.\n   151→\n   152→**Present to user for approval before Phase 3.**\n   153→\n   154→### Phase 3: Release Spec Synthesis\n   155→\n   156→**Phase 2→3 Entry Gate**: Before beginning Phase 3, verify ALL of the following:\n   157→- Phase 2 contract `status: completed`\n   158→- All blocking checks passed (no unresolved BLOCKING gate failures)\n   159→- Phase 2 `step_mapping` contains ≥1 entry\n   160→\n   161→**STOP** if any entry gate condition fails. Report which condition failed and emit the return contract with `failure_phase: 3, failure_type: prerequisite_failed`.\n   162→\n   163→**Goal**: Generate a complete, reviewed release specification from Phase 1 and Phase 2 outputs. Record the wall clock start time for `phase_3_seconds` timing instrumentation.\n   164→\n   165→**Timing**: Record `phase_3_start = current_time()` at the beginning of this phase. NFR-001 advisory target: Phase 3 should complete within 10 minutes wall clock (non-blocking; exceeding this target emits a warning but does not halt the pipeline).\n   166→\n   167→#### Step 3a: Template Instantiation\n   168→\n   169→1. Load the release spec template from `src/superclaude/examples/release-spec-template.md`\n   170→2. Create a working copy at `{work_dir}/portify-release-spec.md`\n   171→3. Verify the working copy was created successfully and contains the template content\n   172→\n   173→#### Step 3b: Content Population\n   174→\n   175→Fill template sections from Phase 1 (workflow analysis) and Phase 2 (pipeline specification) outputs using the following mapping table. For each template section, replace `{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}}` sentinels with content derived from the specified source:\n   176→\n   177→| # | Template Section | Source |\n   178→|---|-----------------|--------|\n   179→| 1 | Problem Statement | Derive from source workflow's purpose + why portification is needed |\n   180→| 2 | Solution Overview | Phase 2 pipeline architecture (step graph, executor design) |\n   181→| 2.2 | Workflow / Data Flow | Phase 1 data flow diagram, adapted for the target pipeline |\n   182→| 3 | Functional Requirements | One FR per generated pipeline step from Phase 2 `step_mapping` — every `step_mapping` entry MUST produce a corresponding FR (SC-004) |\n   183→| 4 | Architecture | Phase 2 `module_plan` (new files), Phase 0 prerequisites (modified files) |\n   184→| 4.5 | Data Models | Phase 2 model designs (Config, Status, Result, MonitorState) |\n   185→| 4.6 | Implementation Order | Phase 2 module dependency order |\n   186→| 5.2 | Gate Criteria | Phase 2 `gate_definitions` |\n   187→| 5.3 | Phase Contracts | Phase 1+2 contract schemas |\n   188→| 6 | NFRs | Standard portification NFRs (sync execution, gate signatures, runner-authored truth) |\n   189→| 7 | Risk Assessment | Derived from Phase 1 classification confidence scores + unsupported patterns |\n   190→| 8 | Test Plan | Structural test plan from Phase 2 pattern coverage matrix |\n   191→| 10 | Downstream Inputs | Themes for sc:roadmap, tasks for sc:tasklist |\n   192→\n   193→After populating all sections, run SC-003 self-validation: verify zero remaining `{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}}` sentinels in the working copy. If any remain, resolve them before proceeding.\n   194→\n   195→#### Step 3c: Automated Brainstorm Pass\n   196→\n   197→Apply embedded brainstorm behavioral patterns as a non-interactive automated pass against the draft spec. This is NOT an invocation of the `sc:brainstorm` command (which is interactive/Socratic); instead, cycle through each of three personas to analyze the draft spec for gaps:\n   198→\n   199→**Persona perspectives** (apply each in sequence):\n   200→\n   201→1. **Architect persona**: Analyze for structural gaps — missing dependencies, incomplete module boundaries, scaling concerns, cross-module interaction gaps, missing error handling paths\n   202→2. **Analyzer persona**: Analyze for logical gaps — uncovered edge cases, ambiguous requirements, missing acceptance criteria, inconsistent cross-references, untestable requirements\n   203→3. **Backend persona**: Analyze for implementation gaps — missing data models, incomplete API contracts, undefined error states, missing retry/timeout specifications, resource lifecycle gaps\n   204→\n   205→**Structured output format**: Each finding uses the schema:\n   206→```\n   207→{gap_id, description, severity(high|medium|low), affected_section, persona}\n   208→```\n   209→\n   210→Example:\n   211→```\n   212→{GAP-001, \"No retry policy defined for failed gate checks\", medium, \"5.2 Gate Criteria\", architect}\n   213→```\n   214→\n   215→**Zero-gap handling**: If no gaps are identified across all three personas, produce an explicit summary:\n   216→> \"No gaps identified by architect, analyzer, and backend personas. Spec coverage assessed as complete.\"\n   217→\n   218→Set `gaps_identified: 0` in the return contract. Zero gaps is a valid outcome — it does not block the pipeline.\n   219→\n   220→**Output**: Append a `## Brainstorm Gap Analysis` section (Section 12) to the draft spec containing all findings in the structured format, or the zero-gap summary.\n   221→\n   222→#### Step 3d: Gap Incorporation\n   223→\n   224→Review each brainstorm finding and route it:\n   225→\n   226→- **Actionable findings** (findings that can be directly addressed): Incorporate into the relevant spec body section identified by `affected_section`. Mark the finding as `[INCORPORATED]` in the gap analysis table.\n   227→- **Unresolvable items** (findings that require external input, are out of scope, or cannot be resolved within the spec): Route to Section 11 (Open Items) with the gap ID, question, impact, and resolution target. Mark the finding as `[OPEN]` in the gap analysis table.\n   228→\n   229→After incorporation, update the gap analysis summary with counts: `{total_gaps, incorporated, open, severity_distribution}`.\n   230→\n   231→**Timing**: Record `phase_3_end = current_time()`. Compute `phase_3_seconds = phase_3_end - phase_3_start`. Populate `phase_timing.phase_3_seconds` in the return contract. If `phase_3_seconds > 600`, emit warning: \"Phase 3 exceeded 10-minute advisory target (NFR-001)\".\n   232→\n   233→**Output**: `{work_dir}/portify-release-spec.md` — Complete draft spec with brainstorm gap analysis section and incorporated findings.\n   234→\n   235→**Phase 3→4 automatic gate**: Draft spec populated (no `{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}}` values remain) AND brainstorm section (Section 12) present in draft spec.\n   236→\n   237→### Phase 4: Spec Panel Review\n   238→\n   239→Embed `sc:spec-panel` behavioral patterns in a convergent review loop. Like Phase 3's brainstorm, this embeds spec-panel behavioral patterns directly rather than invoking the `sc:spec-panel` command (Constraint 1: no inter-skill command invocation).\n   240→\n   241→**Timing**: Record `phase_4_start = current_time()` at the beginning of this phase. NFR-002 advisory target: Phase 4 should complete within 15 minutes wall clock (non-blocking; exceeding this target emits a warning but does not halt the pipeline).\n   242→\n   243→#### Step 4a: Focus Pass\n   244→\n   245→Apply spec-panel behavioral patterns with `--focus correctness,architecture` against `portify-release-spec.md`. This pass embeds four expert analysis patterns inline:\n   246→\n   247→**Expert analysis patterns** (apply each in sequence, building on prior context):\n   248→\n   249→1. **Fowler (Architecture)**: Analyze interface design quality, bounded context boundaries, module coupling/cohesion, dependency direction, and design pattern appropriateness. Annotate data flows with count divergence analysis — for each transformation, document input count, output count, and whether the spec accounts for count differences.\n   250→\n   251→2. **Nygard (Reliability/Failure Modes)**: Analyze failure mode coverage, circuit breaker patterns, timeout specifications, retry policies, and recovery mechanisms. Extend guard boundary analysis to include zero/empty cases — for every guard condition, verify the spec defines behavior for zero, empty, null, and negative inputs.\n   252→\n   253→3. **Whittaker (Adversarial)**: Apply five attack methodologies (Zero/Empty, Divergence, Sentinel Collision, Sequence, Accumulation) against each identified invariant. Produce concrete attack scenarios with state traces demonstrating specification gaps.\n   254→\n   255→4. **Crispin (Testing)**: Analyze testing strategy coverage, acceptance criteria quality, edge case identification, and quality attribute specifications. Generate boundary value test cases for every guard condition and state variable covering: below minimum, at minimum, typical, at maximum, above maximum, and degenerate (zero/empty/null).\n   256→\n   257→**Focus dimensions**: `correctness` and `architecture` — findings must address both dimensions (SC-006).\n   258→\n   259→**Output format**: Each finding uses the structured schema:\n   260→```\n   261→{finding_id, severity(CRITICAL|MAJOR|MINOR), expert, location, issue, recommendation}\n   262→```\n   263→\n   264→Where:\n   265→- `finding_id`: Unique identifier (e.g., `F-001`, `F-002`)\n   266→- `severity`: One of `CRITICAL` (spec provably wrong), `MAJOR` (spec ambiguous/incomplete), `MINOR` (spec could be clearer)\n   267→- `expert`: One of `Fowler`, `Nygard`, `Whittaker`, `Crispin`\n   268→- `location`: Section/requirement reference in the spec\n   269→- `issue`: Description of the identified problem\n   270→- `recommendation`: Specific actionable fix\n   271→\n   272→Additionally produce, if applicable:\n   273→- Guard Condition Boundary Table (Nygard leads, Crispin validates, Whittaker attacks)\n   274→- Pipeline Quantity Flow Diagram (Fowler leads, if pipeline stages detected)\n   275→- State Variable Registry (if 3+ mutable state variables identified)\n   276→\n   277→#### Step 4b: Focus Incorporation\n   278→\n   279→Review focus findings from step 4a. Route each finding by severity level. All modifications MUST be additive-only — append or extend spec sections only, do not rewrite existing content (Constraint 2, NFR-008).\n   280→\n   281→**Severity routing**:\n   282→\n   283→- **CRITICAL findings**: MUST be addressed. Either:\n   284→  - Incorporate the fix into the relevant spec body section identified by `location`, OR\n   285→  - Document a written justification for dismissal in the panel report (Constraint 7). Dismissal justification must include: `finding_id`, reason for dismissal, and assessment of downstream impact.\n   286→- **MAJOR findings**: Incorporate into the corresponding spec body section identified by `location`. Modifications use append/extend only.\n   287→- **MINOR findings**: Append to Section 11 (Open Items) with the `finding_id`, issue summary, and recommended resolution target.\n   288→\n   289→All modifications are traceable by `finding_id` from step 4a output. Mark each finding as `[INCORPORATED]`, `[DISMISSED]`, or `[OPEN]` in the focus findings table.\n   290→\n   291→#### Step 4c: Critique Pass\n   292→\n   293→Apply spec-panel behavioral patterns with `--mode critique` against the updated spec (after step 4b incorporation). Run the full expert panel in critique review sequence:\n   294→\n   295→1. Fowler — Architecture and interface design\n   296→2. Nygard — Reliability and failure mode analysis\n   297→3. Whittaker — Adversarial attack-based specification probing\n   298→4. Crispin — Testing strategy and acceptance criteria\n   299→\n   300→Each expert produces quality dimension scores and prioritized improvement recommendations.\n   301→\n   302→**Quality score output** (SC-007):\n   303→```\n   304→{clarity: float, completeness: float, testability: float, consistency: float}\n   305→```\n   306→\n   307→Where each dimension is a float in the 0-10 range:\n   308→- `clarity` (0.0-10.0): Language precision, unambiguous requirements, clear behavioral definitions\n   309→- `completeness` (0.0-10.0): Coverage of essential elements, no missing requirements, all edge cases addressed\n   310→- `testability` (0.0-10.0): Measurable acceptance criteria, verifiable requirements, concrete test scenarios\n   311→- `consistency` (0.0-10.0): Internal coherence, no contradictions, aligned cross-references\n   312→\n   313→Each expert also produces prioritized improvement recommendations as new findings using the same structured schema from step 4a.\n   314→\n   315→#### Step 4d: Critique Incorporation and Scoring\n   316→\n   317→1. **Record quality scores**: Write all 4 quality dimension scores from step 4c into the spec frontmatter:\n   318→   ```yaml\n   319→   quality_scores:\n   320→     clarity: <float>\n   321→     completeness: <float>\n   322→     testability: <float>\n   323→     consistency: <float>\n   324→     overall: <float>\n   325→   ```\n   326→\n   327→2. **Compute overall score**: `overall = mean(clarity, completeness, testability, consistency)` — that is, `overall = (clarity + completeness + testability + consistency) / 4` (Constraint 6, SC-010).\n   328→\n   329→3. **Append panel report**: Generate `panel-report.md` in the working directory containing:\n   330→   - All focus findings from step 4a with incorporation status\n   331→   - All critique findings from step 4c with scores\n   332→   - Guard Condition Boundary Table (if produced)\n   333→   - Quality dimension scores and overall score\n   334→   - Convergence status\n   335→\n   336→#### Convergence Loop\n   337→\n   338→Steps 4a through 4d execute within a bounded convergence loop using state machine semantics.\n   339→\n   340→**States**:\n   341→- `REVIEWING` — Executing focus pass (4a) or critique pass (4c)\n   342→- `INCORPORATING` — Executing incorporation (4b or 4d)\n   343→- `SCORING` — Evaluating convergence after scoring (4d)\n   344→- `CONVERGED` — Terminal state: zero unaddressed CRITICALs, `status: success`\n   345→- `ESCALATED` — Terminal state: 3 iterations exhausted, `status: partial`\n   346→\n   347→**Transitions**:\n   348→```\n   349→REVIEWING → INCORPORATING (findings produced)\n   350→INCORPORATING → SCORING (incorporation complete, scores computed)\n   351→SCORING → CONVERGED (zero unaddressed CRITICALs remaining)\n   352→SCORING → REVIEWING (unaddressed CRITICALs remain, iteration < 3)\n   353→SCORING → ESCALATED (unaddressed CRITICALs remain, iteration >= 3)\n   354→```\n   355→\n   356→**Iteration counter**: Initialize `iteration = 1` before first pass. Increment after each SCORING → REVIEWING transition. Hard cap: `max_iterations = 3` (SC-008).\n   357→\n   358→**Convergence predicate**: After SCORING, check if any findings with `severity: CRITICAL` have status other than `[INCORPORATED]` or `[DISMISSED]`. If zero unaddressed CRITICALs → transition to CONVERGED. Otherwise → check iteration counter.\n   359→\n   360→**Terminal states**:\n   361→- **CONVERGED**: `status: success` — all CRITICALs addressed, spec is review-complete\n   362→- **ESCALATED**: `status: partial` — 3 iterations exhausted with unaddressed CRITICALs remaining. Escalate to user with: remaining CRITICAL findings, iteration history, and recommendation for manual resolution.\n   363→\n   364→#### Downstream Ready Gate\n   365→\n   366→After convergence loop terminates (either CONVERGED or ESCALATED):\n   367→\n   368→1. Evaluate `downstream_ready` gate: `if overall >= 7.0 then downstream_ready = true else downstream_ready = false` (Constraint 8, SC-012).\n   369→   - Boundary: `overall = 7.0` → `downstream_ready: true`\n   370→   - Boundary: `overall = 6.9` → `downstream_ready: false`\n   371→\n   372→2. **Timing**: Record `phase_4_end = current_time()`. Compute `phase_4_seconds = phase_4_end - phase_4_start`. Populate `phase_timing.phase_4_seconds` in the return contract (SC-013). If `phase_4_seconds > 900`, emit warning: \"Phase 4 exceeded 15-minute advisory target (NFR-002)\".\n   373→\n   374→3. Populate the return contract with:\n   375→   - `convergence_state`: `CONVERGED` or `ESCALATED`\n   376→   - `convergence_iterations`: number of iterations completed\n   377→   - `quality_scores`: all 4 dimensions + overall\n   378→   - `downstream_ready`: boolean\n   379→   - `phase_timing.phase_4_seconds`: elapsed seconds\n   380→\n   381→**Present final spec to user for approval before delivering.**\n   382→\n   383→## Decision Framework: What Becomes Programmatic\n   384→\n   385→The value of portification is moving orchestration out of inference. Here are concrete examples from tested workflows:\n   386→\n   387→| Operation | Classification | Why |\n   388→|-----------|---------------|-----|\n   389→| Enumerate repo files | Pure programmatic | `git ls-files` + pattern matching is deterministic |\n   390→| Classify file domains | Pure programmatic | Extension/path pattern rules, no judgment needed |\n   391→| Assign files to batches | Pure programmatic | Math: divide files by batch_size, assign to agents |\n   392→| Filter Pass 1 results for Pass 2 | Pure programmatic | Parse markdown, extract classifications, filter list |\n   393→| Analyze file for issues | Claude-assisted | Requires reading code, understanding context |\n   394→| Score variant quality | Hybrid | Formulas are programmatic, rubric evaluation is Claude |\n   395→| Validate structural integrity | Pure programmatic | Regex checks, section presence, field counts |\n   396→| Spot-check verification | Claude-assisted | Re-reads files, re-evaluates claims independently |\n   397→| Merge batch reports | Claude-assisted | Requires synthesis, deduplication, pattern extraction |\n   398→| Compute convergence score | Pure programmatic | `agreed / total` — arithmetic |\n   399→| Generate executive summary | Claude-assisted | Requires narrative synthesis |\n   400→\n   401→### Rule of Thumb\n   402→If you can write a Python function for it with clear input→output types and no ambiguity, make it programmatic. If it requires reading natural language, making judgments, or synthesizing information, use Claude with a gate to verify the output.\n   403→\n   404→## Pipeline Design Principles\n   405→\n   406→These inform Phase 2 pipeline specification and align with the unified-audit-gating architecture in `pipeline/`.\n   407→\n   408→### Critical Constraints\n   409→1. **Synchronous execution** — Sprint uses `threading` + `time.sleep()` polling. Use `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch parallelism. Do NOT use async/await.\n   410→2. **Gate function signatures** — All semantic checks return `tuple[bool, str]`. The reason string feeds diagnostics and retry decisions.\n   411→3. **Runner-authored truth** — Reports derive from runner-observed data (exit codes, artifacts, gates, monitor signals), not Claude's self-reported status.\n   412→4. **Deterministic flow control** — Python makes all decisions about what runs next. Claude never decides \"what's next\".\n   413→\n   414→### Shared Pipeline Patterns\n   415→5. **Reuse `pipeline/` primitives** — `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `GateMode`, `GateCriteria`, `SemanticCheck`, `gate_passed()`, `ClaudeProcess`\n   416→6. **Gate modes** — `BLOCKING` (default) stops pipeline on failure. `TRAILING` defers evaluation. Use trailing only for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n   417→7. **Turn budget** — `TurnLedger` with debit/credit/guard for multi-subprocess pipelines. Pre-launch guards prevent launching when budget too low.\n   418→8. **Subprocess isolation** — 4-layer model: scoped work dir, git ceiling, isolated plugin dir, isolated settings dir.\n   419→9. **Context injection** — Prior results serialized into subsequent prompts. Compress older, preserve recent.\n   420→10. **Resume-first failures** — On halt: exact resume command, remaining work, diagnostic path, budget suggestion.\n   421→11. **Diagnostic chain** — Runner-side failure analysis that doesn't consume turn budget.\n   422→\n   423→### Output Guidance\n   424→- `portify-analysis.md`: Under 400 lines. Step graph, gates, data flow.\n   425→- `portify-spec.md`: Split prompts to separate file if spec exceeds 800 lines.\n   426→- Pure-programmatic steps: Full implementation code, not descriptions.\n   427→\n   428→## Boundaries\n   429→\n   430→### Will Do\n   431→- Analyze any SuperClaude skill/command/agent workflow\n   432→- Decompose into structured pipeline specification\n   433→- Generate a complete release specification via template instantiation and brainstorm pass\n   434→- Run spec panel review for quality validation\n   435→- Produce a downstream-ready spec for sc:roadmap and sc:tasklist\n   436→\n   437→### Will Not Do\n   438→- Generate code files directly (use the release spec with sc:implement instead)\n   439→- Execute the generated pipeline\n   440→- Modify original skill/command/agent files\n   441→- Create new skills or agents\n   442→- Make architectural decisions about the workflow's logic\n   443→\n   444→## Return Contract Schema\n   445→\n   446→The return contract is emitted on **every invocation** including success, partial completion, failure, and dry-run (SC-009). All fields are always present; default values apply on failure paths.\n   447→\n   448→### Contract Fields\n   449→\n   450→```yaml\n   451→# --- Identity ---\n   452→contract_version: \"2.0\"            # Schema version for downstream compatibility\n   453→spec_file: \"<path>\"                 # Path to generated release spec (empty string on failure)\n   454→panel_report: \"<path>\"              # Path to panel-report.md (empty string if not produced)\n   455→output_directory: \"<path>\"          # Working directory for all artifacts\n   456→\n   457→# --- Quality Scores (SC-007, SC-010) ---\n   458→quality_scores:\n   459→  clarity: <float>                  # 0.0-10.0 — Language precision, unambiguous requirements\n   460→  completeness: <float>             # 0.0-10.0 — Coverage of essential elements\n   461→  testability: <float>              # 0.0-10.0 — Measurable acceptance criteria\n   462→  consistency: <float>              # 0.0-10.0 — Internal coherence, no contradictions\n   463→  overall: <float>                  # mean(clarity, completeness, testability, consistency) (SC-010)\n   464→\n   465→# --- Convergence ---\n   466→convergence_iterations: <int>       # Number of review loop iterations completed (0 on failure)\n   467→convergence_state: \"<state>\"        # CONVERGED | ESCALATED | NOT_STARTED\n   468→\n   469→# --- Timing (SC-013) ---\n   470→phase_timing:\n   471→  phase_3_seconds: <float>          # Wall clock seconds for Phase 3 (0.0 if not reached)\n   472→  phase_4_seconds: <float>          # Wall clock seconds for Phase 4 (0.0 if not reached)\n   473→\n   474→# --- Pipeline Metadata ---\n   475→source_step_count: <int>            # Number of steps identified in Phase 1 analysis\n   476→spec_fr_count: <int>                # Number of functional requirements in generated spec\n   477→api_snapshot_hash: \"<hash>\"         # SHA-256 of the pipeline-spec.md at time of spec generation\n   478→\n   479→# --- Downstream Readiness (SC-012) ---\n   480→downstream_ready: <bool>            # true if overall >= 7.0, false otherwise\n   481→                                    # Boundary: overall = 7.0 → true; overall = 6.9 → false\n   482→\n   483→# --- Phase Contracts ---\n   484→phase_contracts:\n   485→  phase_0: \"<status>\"               # completed | skipped | failed\n   486→  phase_1: \"<status>\"               # completed | skipped | failed\n   487→  phase_2: \"<status>\"               # completed | skipped | failed\n   488→  phase_3: \"<status>\"               # completed | skipped | failed\n   489→  phase_4: \"<status>\"               # completed | skipped | failed\n   490→\n   491→# --- Warnings ---\n   492→warnings: []                        # List of advisory messages (e.g., timing threshold exceeded)\n   493→\n   494→# --- Failure Information ---\n   495→status: \"<status>\"                  # success | partial | failed | dry_run\n   496→failure_phase: <int|null>           # Phase number where failure occurred (null on success)\n   497→failure_type: \"<type|null>\"         # Failure type enumeration value (null on success)\n   498→\n   499→# --- Resume Support ---\n   500→resume_phase: <int|null>            # Phase to resume from (null if not resumable)\n   501→resume_substep: \"<substep|null>\"    # Substep within phase to resume from (null if not resumable)\n   502→resume_command: \"<command|null>\"    # Full CLI command to resume execution (null if not resumable)\n   503→```\n   504→\n   505→### Failure Type Enumeration\n   506→\n   507→The `failure_type` field uses the following enumeration to classify failures:\n   508→\n   509→| Value | Description | Resumable | Resume Point |\n   510→|-------|-------------|-----------|--------------|\n   511→| `template_failed` | Release spec template could not be loaded or instantiated | No | — |\n   512→| `brainstorm_failed` | Automated brainstorm pass failed to produce findings | Yes | `3c` |\n   513→| `brainstorm_timeout` | Brainstorm pass exceeded time budget | Yes | `3c` |\n   514→| `focus_failed` | Spec panel focus pass failed to produce findings | Yes | `4a` |\n   515→| `critique_failed` | Spec panel critique pass failed to produce scores | Yes | `4a` |\n   516→| `convergence_exhausted` | Maximum convergence iterations (3) reached with unresolved CRITICALs | No | — |\n   517→| `user_rejected` | User rejected spec at an approval gate | No | — |\n   518→| `prerequisite_failed` | Phase entry gate condition not met | No | — |\n   519→\n   520→### Failure Path Defaults (NFR-009)\n   521→\n   522→On any failure path, the contract is emitted with these defaults:\n   523→- All `quality_scores` fields default to `0.0` (NOT null)\n   524→- `downstream_ready` defaults to `false`\n   525→- `convergence_iterations` defaults to `0`\n   526→- `convergence_state` defaults to `NOT_STARTED`\n   527→- `spec_file` and `panel_report` default to `\"\"` (empty string)\n   528→- `resume_substep` is populated for resumable failure types; null otherwise\n   529→- All `phase_contracts` entries for incomplete phases default to `failed`\n   530→- `phase_timing` entries default to `0.0` for phases not reached\n   531→\n   532→### Resume Behavior Semantics\n   533→\n   534→**Phase 3 Resume** (`resume_substep=3c`):\n   535→- Preserves the populated spec from Step 3b (template instantiation + content population)\n   536→- Brainstorm pass (Step 3c) re-runs from scratch against the preserved draft\n   537→- Gap incorporation (Step 3d) re-runs after brainstorm completes\n   538→- All Phase 1 and Phase 2 artifacts are preserved unchanged\n   539→\n   540→**Phase 4 Resume** (`resume_substep=4a`):\n   541→- Preserves the complete draft spec from Phase 3 (including brainstorm findings)\n   542→- Focus pass (Step 4a) re-runs from scratch against the preserved spec\n   543→- All subsequent steps (4b, 4c, 4d) re-run in sequence\n   544→- Convergence loop resets iteration counter to 1\n   545→- All Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 artifacts are preserved unchanged\n   546→\n   547→### Contract Emission on Dry Run\n   548→\n   549→When `--dry-run` is active, the contract is emitted with:\n   550→- `status: dry_run`\n   551→- Phases 0-2 contracts populated; Phases 3-4 marked as `skipped`\n   552→- All quality scores set to `0.0`\n   553→- `downstream_ready: false`\n   554→- `convergence_state: NOT_STARTED`\n   555→- No spec synthesis or panel review artifacts produced\n   556→\n   557→## Related Commands\n   558→\n   559→- `/sc:implement` — General feature implementation\n   560→- `/sc:design` — System architecture design\n   561→- `/sc:analyze` — Code analysis for understanding workflows\n   562→- `/sc:build` — Build and compile after generating\n   563→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01W2ak1ngtyEbtE9kCCQMLTt","session_id":"34084fba-c3f1-4af9-ac87-2e7dffccedcc","uuid":"c12d2d4b-835a-44b8-bd17-8724f51e14f4"}

Fill in all `{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}}` values. Remove sections marked `[CONDITIONAL: ...]` if not applicable to your spec type. Delete this usage block from your final spec.\n     4→>\n     5→> **Spec types supported**: New feature, refactoring, portification, infrastructure\n     6→>\n     7→> **Conditional sections by spec type**:\n     8→> - **Portification**: Include Section 9 (Migration & Rollout), Section 5 (Interface Contracts)\n     9→> - **Refactoring**: Include Section 4.3 (Removed Files), Section 9 (Migration & Rollout)\n    10→> - **New feature**: Include Section 4.5 (Data Models), Section 5.1 (CLI Surface)\n    11→> - **Infrastructure**: Include Section 5.3 (Phase Contracts), Section 8.3 (Manual/E2E Tests)\n    12→>\n    13→> **Quality gate**: Before this spec is considered complete, it should pass `/sc:spec-panel --focus correctness,architecture` and `/sc:spec-panel --mode critique`.\n    14→>\n    15→> **Sentinel self-check**: After populating, verify zero remaining sentinels: `grep -c '{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:' <output-file>` should return 0.\n    16→\n    17→---\n    18→\n    19→```yaml\n    20→---\n    21→title: \"{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:spec_title}}\"\n    22→version: \"1.0.0\"\n    23→status: draft\n    24→feature_id: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:fr_id}}\n    25→parent_feature: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:parent_feature_or_null}}\n    26→spec_type: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:new_feature_or_refactoring_or_portification_or_infrastructure}}\n    27→complexity_score: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_1.0}}\n    28→complexity_class: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:simple_or_moderate_or_high}}\n    29→target_release: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:version}}\n    30→authors: [user, claude]\n    31→created: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:yyyy_mm_dd}}\n    32→quality_scores:\n    33→  clarity: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_10.0}}\n    34→  completeness: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_10.0}}\n    35→  testability: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_10.0}}\n    36→  consistency: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_10.0}}\n    37→  overall: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:0.0_to_10.0}}\n    38→---\n    39→```\n    40→\n    41→## 1. Problem Statement\n    42→\n    43→> What problem does this work solve? Why does it matter? What fails or is suboptimal today?\n    44→\n    45→{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:problem_description}}\n    46→\n    47→### 1.1 Evidence\n    48→\n    49→> Concrete evidence that the problem exists. Links to issues, failing tests, user reports, forensic findings.\n    50→\n    51→| Evidence | Source | Impact |\n    52→|----------|--------|--------|\n    53→| {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:evidence_1}} | {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:source}} | {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:impact}} |\n    54→\n    55→### 1.2 Scope Boundary\n    56→\n    57→> What this spec addresses and explicitly does NOT address.\n    58→\n    59→**In scope**: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:in_scope}}\n    60→\n    61→**Out of scope**: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:out_of_scope}}\n    62→\n    63→## 2. Solution Overview\n    64→\n    65→> High-level description of the approach. What changes, what stays the same.\n    66→\n    67→{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:solution_overview}}\n    68→\n    69→### 2.1 Key Design Decisions\n    70→\n    71→> Decisions made during brainstorming/design that shaped this spec. Each decision should have a rationale.\n    72→\n    73→| Decision | Choice | Alternatives Considered | Rationale |\n    74→|----------|--------|------------------------|-----------|\n    75→| {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:decision_1}} | {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:choice}} | {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:alternatives}} | {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:rationale}} |\n    76→\n    77→### 2.2 Workflow / Data Flow\n    78→\n    79→> How the system works end-to-end after this change. Use ASCII diagrams for pipeline flows.\n    80→\n    81→```\n    82→{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:flow_diagram}}\n    83→```\n    84→\n    85→## 3. Functional Requirements\n    86→\n    87→> Numbered requirements. Each must be testable and traceable.\n    88→\n    89→### FR-{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:id}}.1: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:requirement_title}}\n    90→\n    91→**Description**: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:what_it_does}}\n    92→\n    93→**Acceptance Criteria**:\n    94→- [ ] {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:criterion_1}}\n    95→- [ ] {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:criterion_2}}\n    96→\n    97→**Dependencies**: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:dependencies_or_none}}\n    98→\n    99→### FR-{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:id}}.2: {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:requirement_title}}\n   100→\n   101→{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:repeat_pattern}}\n   102→\n   103→## 4. Architecture\n   104→\n   105→### 4.1 New Files\n   106→\n   107→> Files created by this work. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011YRPZxTSjo6GYwTJoehfHr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Pipeline Specification Reference\n     2→\n     3→Loaded during Phase 2 of portification. This ref provides the detailed specification patterns for converting a workflow analysis into concrete code designs.\n     4→\n     5→## Phase 2→3 Bridge\n     6→\n     7→Phase 2 outputs (`portify-spec.md` and optional `portify-prompts.md`) flow into Phase 3 (Release Spec Synthesis), NOT into code generation. The bridge works as follows:\n     8→\n     9→1. **Phase 2 produces**: Step graph, model designs, gate definitions, executor patterns, pure-programmatic implementations, and integration plan — all as specification documents.\n    10→2. **Phase 3 consumes**: Phase 2 outputs are mapped into a release specification template. Each Phase 2 step becomes a functional requirement (FR). Model designs populate the data models section. Gate definitions populate the gate criteria section.\n    11→3. **Phase 3→4 gate**: The synthesized spec must have zero remaining `{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}}` sentinels and a brainstorm gap analysis section before entering Phase 4 (Spec Panel Review).\n    12→\n    13→Phase 2 does NOT produce runnable code. It produces code-ready specifications that inform the release spec, which is then reviewed by the panel in Phase 4 and ultimately consumed by `sc:roadmap` and `sc:tasklist` for implementation planning.\n    14→\n    15→## Step Graph Design\n    16→\n    17→### Step Definition Pattern\n    18→\n    19→Every workflow operation that produces an artifact maps to a `Step`:\n    20→\n    21→```python\n    22→Step(\n    23→    id=\"step-id\",              # kebab-case, unique within pipeline\n    24→    prompt=build_X_prompt(),   # Function from prompts.py\n    25→    output_file=\"artifact.md\", # Relative to work_dir\n    26→    gate=STEP_GATE,            # From gates.py\n    27→    gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,  # BLOCKING (default) or TRAILING\n    28→    timeout_seconds=600,       # Per-step timeout\n    29→    inputs=[\"dep1.md\"],        # Files this step reads\n    30→    retry_limit=1,             # How many retries on gate failure\n    31→    model=None,                # None = use default from config\n    32→)\n    33→```\n    34→\n    35→### Gate Mode Selection\n    36→\n    37→Choose `GateMode` based on step criticality:\n    38→- `BLOCKING` (default): Pipeline halts if gate fails. Use for steps whose output is consumed by downstream steps.\n    39→- `TRAILING`: Gate evaluates asynchronously; pipeline continues. Use for quality checks that don't affect downstream data flow.\n    40→\n    41→Rule of thumb from `resolve_gate_mode()`:\n    42→- Release scope → always BLOCKING\n    43→- Milestone scope → configurable\n    44→- Task scope → TRAILING when `grace_period > 0`\n    45→\n    46→### Parallel Group Pattern\n    47→\n    48→Steps that can run concurrently are wrapped in a list:\n    49→\n    50→```python\n    51→steps = [\n    52→    single_step_1,\n    53→    [parallel_step_a, parallel_step_b],  # Run concurrently\n    54→    single_step_2,                        # Waits for parallel group\n    55→]\n    56→```\n    57→\n    58→The generic `execute_pipeline()` handles thread coordination. If using sprint-style custom executor, implement your own parallel dispatch.\n    59→\n    60→### Dependency Rules\n    61→\n    62→- Steps consume outputs of prior steps via `inputs` list\n    63→- A step can only reference outputs from steps that precede it in the graph\n    64→- Parallel steps within a group must not depend on each other\n    65→- Resume logic skips steps whose output already passes their gate\n    66→\n    67→## Model Design Patterns\n    68→\n    69→### Config Model\n    70→\n    71→Always extend `PipelineConfig`:\n    72→\n    73→```python\n    74→@dataclass\n    75→class MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n    76→    \"\"\"Pipeline-specific configuration.\"\"\"\n    77→    # Required: identify the input\n    78→    source_path: Path\n    79→\n    80→    # Optional: control behavior\n    81→    depth: str = \"standard\"       # quick|standard|deep\n    82→\n    83→    # Computed paths\n    84→    @property\n    85→    def results_dir(self) -> Path:\n    86→        return self.work_dir / \"results\"\n    87→\n    88→    @property\n    89→    def output_file(self, step_id: str) -> Path:\n    90→        return self.work_dir / f\"{step_id}-output.md\"\n    91→```\n    92→\n    93→### Status Enum\n    94→\n    95→Sprint-style pipelines need richer status than generic `StepStatus`:\n    96→\n    97→```python\n    98→class StepPhaseStatus(Enum):\n    99→    PENDING = \"pending\"\n   100→    RUNNING = \"running\"\n   101→    PASS = \"pass\"\n   102→    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"    # Completed but no structured report\n   103→    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"    # Output exists but no result file\n   104→    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"             # Budget exhausted\n   105→    HALT = \"halt\"                         # Step recommends stopping\n   106→    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"                   # Hard timeout exceeded\n   107→    ERROR = \"error\"                       # Process crash\n   108→    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"                   # Skipped by config\n   109→```\n   110→\n   111→### Result Model\n   112→\n   113→Extend `StepResult` with domain telemetry:\n   114→\n   115→```python\n   116→@dataclass\n   117→class MyStepResult(StepResult):\n   118→    exit_code: int | None = None\n   119→    started_at: float | None = None\n   120→    finished_at: float | None = None\n   121→    output_bytes: int = 0\n   122→    error_bytes: int = 0\n   123→    # Domain-specific fields:\n   124→    artifacts_produced: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n   125→    gate_details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n   126→```\n   127→\n   128→### Monitor State\n   129→\n   130→Domain-specific signals extracted from NDJSON output:\n   131→\n   132→```python\n   133→@dataclass\n   134→class MyMonitorState:\n   135→    output_bytes: int = 0\n   136→    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n   137→    events_received: int = 0\n   138→    lines_total: int = 0\n   139→    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n   140→    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n   141→    # Domain-specific signals:\n   142→    last_step_id: str | None = None\n   143→    current_artifact: str | None = None\n   144→    files_changed: set = field(default_factory=set)\n   145→```\n   146→\n   147→## Gate Design Patterns\n   148→\n   149→### Gate Criteria Tiers\n   150→\n   151→```python\n   152→# Exempt: always passes (for optional/informational steps)\n   153→EXEMPT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   154→    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n   155→    min_lines=0,\n   156→    enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n   157→)\n   158→\n   159→# Light: file exists and non-empty\n   160→LIGHT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   161→    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n   162→    min_lines=0,\n   163→    enforcement_tier=\"LIGHT\",\n   164→)\n   165→\n   166→# Standard: structural validation\n   167→STANDARD_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   168→    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\"],\n   169→    min_lines=50,\n   170→    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n   171→)\n   172→\n   173→# Strict: structural + semantic validation\n   174→STRICT_GATE = GateCriteria(\n   175→    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"title\", \"status\", \"convergence_score\"],\n   176→    min_lines=100,\n   177→    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n   178→    semantic_checks=[\n   179→        SemanticCheck(\n   180→            name=\"no_heading_gaps\",\n   181→            check_fn=_no_heading_gaps,\n   182→            failure_message=\"Heading hierarchy has gaps (e.g. H1→H3 without H2)\",\n   183→        ),\n   184→        SemanticCheck(\n   185→            name=\"refs_resolve\",\n   186→            check_fn=_cross_refs_resolve,\n   187→            failure_message=\"Cross-references do not resolve to valid targets\",\n   188→        ),\n   189→    ],\n   190→)\n   191→```\n   192→\n   193→### Semantic Check Functions\n   194→\n   195→Semantic checks are pure functions matching the `Callable[[str], bool]` signature — they take file content and return a boolean:\n   196→\n   197→```python\n   198→def _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n   199→    \"\"\"Verify heading hierarchy has no gaps (H1->H3 without H2).\"\"\"\n   200→    # ... implementation ...\n   201→    return True  # or False on failure\n   202→\n   203→def _has_required_sections(content: str) -> bool:\n   204→    \"\"\"Verify all required sections are present.\"\"\"\n   205→    required = {\"## Summary\", \"## Findings\", \"## Recommendations\"}\n   206→    present = {line.strip() for line in content.splitlines() if line.startswith(\"## \")}\n   207→    missing = required - present\n   208→    return len(missing) == 0\n   209→```\n   210→\n   211→### Frontmatter Validation\n   212→\n   213→The shared `gate_passed()` function handles YAML frontmatter checks. Steps that produce markdown with frontmatter get automatic validation:\n   214→\n   215→```yaml\n   216→---\n   217→title: Diff Analysis\n   218→status: complete\n   219→diff_points: 12\n   220→convergence_ready: true\n   221→---\n   222→```\n   223→\n   224→## Prompt Design Patterns\n   225→\n   226→### Prompt Builder Structure\n   227→\n   228→Each step's prompt is a function that takes inputs and returns a complete prompt string:\n   229→\n   230→```python\n   231→def build_analysis_prompt(source_path: Path, config: MyConfig) -> str:\n   232→    return f\"\"\"Read the source file and produce a structured analysis.\n   233→\n   234→Source: {source_path}\n   235→Depth: {config.depth}\n   236→\n   237→## Required Output Format\n   238→\n   239→Write your analysis to the output file with this structure:\n   240→\n   241→```yaml\n   242→---\n   243→title: Analysis Report\n   244→status: complete\n   245→finding_count: <integer>\n   246→---\n   247→```\n   248→\n   249→## Sections Required\n   250→\n   251→### Summary\n   252→Brief overview of findings.\n   253→\n   254→### Findings\n   255→Numbered list of findings with evidence.\n   256→\n   257→### Recommendations\n   258→Actionable next steps.\n   259→\n   260→## Machine-Readable Markers\n   261→\n   262→At the end of your output, include:\n   263→```\n   264→EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\n   265→```\n   266→or\n   267→```\n   268→EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\n   269→```\n   270→\"\"\"\n   271→```\n   272→\n   273→### Key Prompt Rules\n   274→\n   275→1. **Specify output file format** — Tell Claude exactly what frontmatter fields to include\n   276→2. **Require machine-readable markers** — `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE|HALT` for status determination\n   277→3. **Embed input content when small** — Under ~50KB, embed directly in prompt; otherwise use `--file` args\n   278→4. **Be explicit about sections** — Name every required section; don't leave structure to inference\n   279→5. **Include depth/focus constraints** — If the step has configurable depth, encode it in the prompt\n   280→\n   281→### Input Embedding Pattern\n   282→\n   283→```python\n   284→def _embed_inputs(prompt: str, inputs: list[Path], config: MyConfig) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:\n   285→    \"\"\"Embed small input files directly into prompt, return file args for large ones.\"\"\"\n   286→    file_args = []\n   287→    for input_path in inputs:\n   288→        full_path = config.work_dir / input_path\n   289→        if full_path.stat().st_size < 50_000:\n   290→            content = full_path.read_text()\n   291→            prompt += f\"\\n\\n---\\n## Input: {input_path}\\n\\n{content}\"\n   292→        else:\n   293→            file_args.append(str(full_path))\n   294→    return prompt, file_args\n   295→```\n   296→\n   297→## Executor Design Patterns\n   298→\n   299→### Sprint-Style Supervisor Loop\n   300→\n   301→The sprint executor pattern provides supervised execution with monitoring. IMPORTANT: Sprint uses synchronous execution with `threading` for parallelism, NOT `async/await`. Use `time.sleep()` polling loops and `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` for batch dispatch.\n   302→\n   303→```python\n   304→def execute_pipeline(config: MyConfig) -> MyResult:\n   305→    # 1. Pre-flight checks\n   306→    verify_claude_binary()\n   307→\n   308→    # 2. Setup infrastructure\n   309→    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   310→    logger = MyLogger(config)\n   311→    tui = MyTUI(config)\n   312→    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n   313→    result = MyResult(config=config)\n   314→    ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=config.max_turns)\n   315→\n   316→    # 3. Start TUI\n   317→    tui.start()\n   318→\n   319→    try:\n   320→        for step in config.steps:\n   321→            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   322→                break\n   323→\n   324→            # 4. Reset monitor for this step\n   325→            monitor.reset(config.output_file(step))\n   326→            monitor.start()\n   327→\n   328→            # 5. Launch Claude subprocess\n   329→            process = ClaudeProcess(config, step)\n   330→            process.start()\n   331→\n   332→            # 6. Supervision loop\n   333→            while process.is_running():\n   334→                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   335→                    process.stop()\n   336→                    break\n   337→\n   338→                state = monitor.get_state()\n   339→\n   340→                # Stall detection\n   341→                if state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout:\n   342→                    process.stop()\n   343→                    break\n   344→\n   345→                # TUI update\n   346→                tui.update(step, state)\n   347→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   348→\n   349→            # 7. Classify result\n   350→            monitor.stop()\n   351→            status = determine_status(process.exit_code, step, config)\n   352→\n   353→            # 8. Record and decide\n   354→            step_result = build_result(step, status, monitor.state)\n   355→            result.add(step_result)\n   356→            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n   357→\n   358→            if status not in (PASS, PASS_NO_SIGNAL):\n   359→                # Collect diagnostics and halt\n   360→                diagnostics = collect_diagnostics(step_result, monitor.state)\n   361→                write_diagnostic_report(diagnostics, config)\n   362→                result.outcome = HALTED\n   363→                break\n   364→\n   365→    finally:\n   366→        tui.stop()\n   367→        monitor.stop()\n   368→        signal_handler.restore()\n   369→\n   370→    return result\n   371→```\n   372→\n   373→### Status Classification\n   374→\n   375→Map every possible exit condition to a deterministic status:\n   376→\n   377→```python\n   378→def determine_status(exit_code, step, config):\n   379→    # 1. Hard failures\n   380→    if exit_code == 124:\n   381→        return TIMEOUT\n   382→    if exit_code != 0:\n   383→        return ERROR\n   384→\n   385→    # 2. Check for structured result file\n   386→    result_path = config.result_file(step)\n   387→    if result_path.exists():\n   388→        content = result_path.read_text()\n   389→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n   390→            return HALT\n   391→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n   392→            return PASS\n   393→        return PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   394→\n   395→    # 3. Check for output without result\n   396→    output_path = config.output_file(step)\n   397→    if output_path.exists():\n   398→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n   399→            return INCOMPLETE\n   400→        return PASS_NO_REPORT\n   401→\n   402→    # 4. No output at all\n   403→    return ERROR\n   404→```\n   405→\n   406→## Integration Patterns\n   407→\n   408→### main.py Registration\n   409→\n   410→```python\n   411→# In src/superclaude/cli/main.py\n   412→from superclaude.cli.my_pipeline import my_pipeline_group\n   413→app.add_command(my_pipeline_group)\n   414→```\n   415→\n   416→### Click Command Group\n   417→\n   418→```python\n   419→@click.group(\"my-pipeline\")\n   420→def my_pipeline_group():\n   421→    \"\"\"My pipeline description.\"\"\"\n   422→    pass\n   423→\n   424→@my_pipeline_group.command(\"run\")\n   425→@click.argument(\"source\")\n   426→@click.option(\"--depth\", default=\"standard\", type=click.Choice([\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]))\n   427→@click.option(\"--dry-run\", is_flag=True)\n   428→@click.option(\"--max-turns\", default=25, type=int)\n   429→@click.option(\"--model\", default=None)\n   430→@click.option(\"--debug\", is_flag=True)\n   431→def run(source, depth, dry_run, max_turns, model, debug):\n   432→    \"\"\"Execute the pipeline.\"\"\"\n   433→    config = load_config(source, depth=depth, dry_run=dry_run, ...)\n   434→    result = execute_pipeline(config)\n   435→    sys.exit(0 if result.success else 1)\n   436→```\n   437→\n   438→## Turn Budget Economics (from unified-audit-gating)\n   439→\n   440→For pipelines with multiple Claude subprocesses, use `TurnLedger` to manage budget:\n   441→\n   442→```python\n   443→from superclaude.cli.sprint.models import TurnLedger\n   444→\n   445→@dataclass\n   446→class MyConfig(PipelineConfig):\n   447→    max_turns: int = 200\n   448→    min_launch_allocation: int = 10\n   449→    min_remediation_budget: int = 5\n   450→\n   451→# In executor:\n   452→ledger = TurnLedger(\n   453→    initial_budget=config.max_turns,\n   454→    minimum_allocation=config.min_launch_allocation,\n   455→    minimum_remediation_budget=config.min_remediation_budget,\n   456→)\n   457→\n   458→# Before launching a step:\n   459→if not ledger.can_launch():\n   460→    result.outcome = HALTED\n   461→    result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted\"\n   462→    break\n   463→\n   464→# After step completes, count actual turns used:\n   465→actual_turns = count_turns_from_output(output_path)\n   466→ledger.debit(actual_turns)\n   467→\n   468→# If step used fewer turns than allocated, credit back:\n   469→if actual_turns < allocated:\n   470→    ledger.credit(allocated - actual_turns)\n   471→```\n   472→\n   473→## Subprocess Isolation Pattern\n   474→\n   475→For pipelines spawning child Claude sessions, use the 4-layer isolation model:\n   476→\n   477→```python\n   478→def setup_isolation(work_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n   479→    \"\"\"Create isolated environment for child Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n   480→    env = os.environ.copy()\n   481→\n   482→    # Layer 1: Scoped work directory\n   483→    env[\"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\"] = str(work_dir)\n   484→\n   485→    # Layer 2: Git ceiling (prevent escaping work_dir)\n   486→    env[\"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\"] = str(work_dir.parent)\n   487→\n   488→    # Layer 3: Isolated plugin directory\n   489→    plugin_dir = work_dir / \".claude-plugins\"\n   490→    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   491→    env[\"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\"] = str(plugin_dir)\n   492→\n   493→    # Layer 4: Isolated settings\n   494→    settings_dir = work_dir / \".claude-settings\"\n   495→    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   496→    env[\"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\"] = str(settings_dir)\n   497→\n   498→    return env\n   499→```\n   500→\n   501→## Context Injection Pattern\n   502→\n   503→Prior step results feed into subsequent prompts as compressed context:\n   504→\n   505→```python\n   506→def build_step_prompt_with_context(step, prior_results: list[StepResult]) -> str:\n   507→    \"\"\"Build prompt with prior step context injected.\"\"\"\n   508→    base_prompt = step.prompt\n   509→\n   510→    if not prior_results:\n   511→        return base_prompt\n   512→\n   513→    context_block = \"## Prior Step Context\\n\\n\"\n   514→    for result in prior_results[-3:]:  # Keep recent, compress older\n   515→        context_block += result.to_context_summary(verbose=(result == prior_results[-1]))\n   516→        context_block += \"\\n\\n\"\n   517→\n   518→    return f\"{base_prompt}\\n\\n{context_block}\"\n   519→```\n   520→\n   521→## Resume-First Failure Output Pattern\n   522→\n   523→On halt, generate actionable resume information:\n   524→\n   525→```python\n   526→@dataclass\n   527→class MyResult:\n   528→    # ... other fields ...\n   529→\n   530→    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n   531→        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from failure point.\"\"\"\n   532→        if not self.halt_step:\n   533→            return None\n   534→        remaining = [r.step.id for r in self.step_results\n   535→                     if r.status == MyStatus.PENDING]\n   536→        return (\n   537→            f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run \"\n   538→            f\"--resume --start {self.halt_step} \"\n   539→            f\"--max-turns {self.suggested_resume_budget}\"\n   540→        )\n   541→\n   542→    @property\n   543→    def suggested_resume_budget(self) -> int:\n   544→        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed to complete remaining work.\"\"\"\n   545→        remaining_steps = sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n   546→                              if r.status in (MyStatus.PENDING, MyStatus.INCOMPLETE))\n   547→        return remaining_steps * 25  # Default allocation per step\n   548→```\n   549→\n   550→## Pure-Programmatic Step Execution\n   551→\n   552→Not all steps need Claude. Pure-programmatic steps run as direct Python function calls:\n   553→\n   554→```python\n   555→def execute_step(step, config):\n   556→    \"\"\"Route step to appropriate executor.\"\"\"\n   557→    if step.prompt == \"\":\n   558→        # Pure programmatic — run directly\n   559→        return run_programmatic_step(step, config)\n   560→    else:\n   561→        # Claude-assisted — launch subprocess\n   562→        return run_claude_step(step, config)\n   563→\n   564→def run_programmatic_step(step, config) -> StepResult:\n   565→    \"\"\"Execute a pure-programmatic step without Claude.\"\"\"\n   566→    started = time.time()\n   567→    try:\n   568→        # Call the step's implementation function\n   569→        PROGRAMMATIC_RUNNERS[step.id](config)\n   570→        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   571→                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n   572→    except Exception as e:\n   573→        return StepResult(step=step, status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   574→                         gate_failure_reason=str(e),\n   575→                         started_at=started, finished_at=time.time())\n   576→```\n   577→\n   578→## Gate Display State Machine (for TUI)\n   579→\n   580→Model gate states as explicit transitions for richer UX:\n   581→\n   582→```python\n   583→class GateDisplayState(Enum):\n   584→    NONE = \"none\"\n   585→    CHECKING = \"checking\"\n   586→    PASS = \"pass\"\n   587→    FAIL_DEFERRED = \"fail_deferred\"\n   588→    REMEDIATING = \"remediating\"\n   589→    REMEDIATED = \"remediated\"\n   590→    HALT = \"halt\"\n   591→\n   592→    @property\n   593→    def icon(self) -> str:\n   594→        return {\n   595→            \"none\": \" \", \"checking\": \"...\",\n   596→            \"pass\": \"ok\", \"fail_deferred\": \"!\",\n   597→            \"remediating\": \"~\", \"remediated\": \"ok\",\n   598→            \"halt\": \"X\",\n   599→        }[self.value]\n   600→```\n   601→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01W2ak1ngtyEbtE9kCCQMLTt","session_id":"34084fba-c3f1-4af9-ac87-2e7dffccedcc","uuid":"cf349704-bb64-411c-8a6b-2492b17a7285"}
